news.Title: 統一期貨：台積電、台達電領漲，台指期開高走高，漲超800點
news.Content: 【財訊快報／編輯部】Nvidia即將於週四凌晨發布財報，於財報發布前美股延續高檔盤整行情，市場情緒觀望。惟台指期夜盤仍以小漲作收，日盤則在台積電、台達電的領漲下開高走高，漲超800點，盤勢表現顯著強於美股。綜觀法人籌碼，三大法人合計買超836.9億元，其中外資買超627.3億元，而自營商買超153.5億元，投信則買超56億元。檢視選擇權金流，外資布局0.58億元偏多部位，自營商則布局2.64億元偏空部位。選擇權未平倉部分，2月W2週選價平近9檔賣權最大未平倉上升600點至34,300點，買權最大未平倉上升800點至35,000點，最大OI區間上移偏多。期貨方面，外資夜盤減碼1,865口空單，日盤則減碼826口空單，未平倉淨空單降至-28,461口；十大特定法人減碼2,167口空單至-1,362口。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台指期24日延續多頭行情，開高走高，漲超800點，表現顯著強於美股。
• 三大法人合計買超836.9億元，其中外資買超627.3億元，自營商買超153.5億元。
• 外資在選擇權布局偏多部位，最大OI區間上移，顯示對後市看法樂觀。
• 關注輝達財報公布，將牽動AI產業前景及台股後續動能。
news.Keywords: 台指期, AI財報, 外資籌碼, 多頭行情, 科技趨勢

news.Title: Where Will Nvidia Stock Be in 2030?
news.Content:
In late 2022, Wall Street noticed that the first iteration of OpenAI's AI chatbot, ChatGPT, was trained and powered by thousands of Nvidia 's (NASDAQ: NVDA) data center graphics processing units (GPUs). And a spark was lit that quickly made the cutting-edge chipmaker the largest company in the world, with a market cap of $4.55 trillion.

The good news is that companies continue to clamor for Nvidia's hardware. But how sustainable is the demand propping up Nvidia's huge valuation? Let's dig deeper into what could come next as the chipmaker seeks to maintain its dominance over the next decade and beyond.

Where to invest $1,000 right now? Our analyst team just revealed what they believe are the 10 best stocks to buy right now, when you join Stock Advisor. See the stocks »

As of early 2026, the AI race shows no signs of slowing down. This month, cloud computing giant Amazon announced plans to increase its full-year capital expenditures by 50% to $200 billion, with much of that going to data center spending. Alphabet has similar goals, with $175 billion to $185 billion earmarked this year. CNBC estimates that total AI spending could hit an eye-popping $700 billion this year alone.

The big-spending hyperscalers can afford to pour so much money into AI because of their diversified and highly profitable businesses. But that doesn't necessarily mean it's a good idea. Capital expenditures come with an opportunity cost because they represent cash flow that could have been used for other things or returned to shareholders through buybacks or dividends. Meanwhile, the value of the AI spending looks uncertain.

While AI technology continues to improve at a rapid rate, it still consistently underperforms humans on basic labor tasks and generates huge losses for the consumer-facing companies like OpenAI and Anthropic that rent computing power from Nvidia's clients. Investors are also starting to balk at the data center spending, with Amazon's share price falling almost 20% in the week following its capex announcement.

Ultimately, public companies exist to serve their shareholders. And if shareholders start consistently punishing Nvidia's clients for their huge AI data center spending, it could force them to cut back or turn to cheaper, homegrown solutions like custom chips.

Nvidia earned around 90% of its revenue from its data center segment, which means the company is extremely overexposed to a potential slowdown in this market. However, over the coming years or decades, management aims to pioneer new markets for its hardware.

Nvidia has already begun aggressively investing in quantum computing . And in October, it announced NVQlink, an architecture designed to couple its GPUs with quantum computers. It is too early to know how things will play out, but analysts expect quantum computing to be commercially viable by the end of the decade. And Nvidia could be positioned to dominate the opportunity because of its expertise in chip design and partnerships with advanced manufacturing partners like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing .

Nvidia also has an opportunity in automotive hardware and robotics, where it provides chips to assist with self-driving cars. This segment represented a relatively modest $592 million in third-quarter revenue, but it grew by 32% from the prior-year period. And there is plenty of room for continued acceleration as self-driving becomes more mainstream.

Over the next decade, new opportunities like quantum computing and automotive could help make up for a possible slowdown in AI data center demand. But with around 90% of Nvidia's sales coming from its data center segment, new revenue streams could struggle to move the needle.

That said, the company's current valuation seems to price in these long-term challenges. With a forward price-to-earnings (P/E) multiple of just 24, the stock is very cheap for a company that grew earnings per share by 67% year over year to $1.30 in its most recent quarter. The low valuation gives the stock a margin of safety as it navigates these uncertain times. Nvidia looks like a hold until more information becomes available.
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia's (NVDA) market cap reached $4.55 trillion, driven by AI demand.
• Hyperscalers like Amazon and Alphabet are significantly increasing AI capital expenditures, totaling an estimated $700 billion this year.
• Nvidia earns ~90% of its revenue from its data center segment, making it exposed to potential slowdowns.
• The company is investing in new markets like quantum computing and automotive hardware.
• Nvidia's forward P/E is 24, with EPS growth of 67% YoY in the latest quarter, suggesting a potentially undervalued stock.
news.Keywords: AI Infrastructure, Data Center Demand, Capital Expenditures, Emerging Markets, Valuation

news.Title: Tesla's Optimus Robot Could Reach Human-Level Proficiency in 2026 -- Time to Buy?
news.Content:
For much of its history, Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) was known for its lineup of electric vehicles (EV) and energy storage products. But over the last several years, CEO Elon Musk has been sharing his vision to transform the company from a pioneer of the sustainable energy movement into a technology-enabled services business driven by artificial intelligence (AI).

At the forefront of this mission is Tesla's humanoid robot, Optimus. Musk is so serious about Optimus' potential that he's made the executive decision to scale down production of the models S and X vehicles and repurpose Tesla's Fremont factory into a manufacturing center for Optimus.

Where to invest $1,000 right now? Our analyst team just revealed what they believe are the 10 best stocks to buy right now, when you join Stock Advisor. See the stocks »

Let's explore what's at stake with Optimus in 2026. With Tesla essentially mortgaging its future on next-generation services, is now the time to buy the stock before AI fuels its next parabolic run?

Tesla has been building, testing, and iterating Optimus for many years. In its current form, the humanoid is known as Optimus Gen 3.

In marketing videos, Tesla claims that Optimus has learned fine motor skills given its ability to handle delicate objects such as tearing paper towels, opening a cabinet, or folding laundry.

In addition, Tesla has published videos of Optimus demonstrating advanced dexterity -- with an ability to catch moving objects tossed in its general direction, as well as learning how to walk on uneven terrain.

During Tesla's fourth-quarter earnings call, Musk said that his long-term goal is to produce 1 million Optimus bots per year at the Fremont factory.

With that said, Musk tempered expectations by telling investors that even with Optimus' advances, the humanoid remains in a research and development (R&D) phase. Besides the lab, Optimus is only deployed within Tesla's own factories so that it can watch, observe, and learn how to complete basic tasks.

Musk hinted that production of Optimus won't occur until "probably the end of this year."

As of this writing (Feb. 18), Tesla boasts a market capitalization of $1.6 trillion. From a valuation perspective, the company trades at a forward price-to-earnings (P/E) multiple of 202. Using Wall Street's consensus earnings per share (EPS) estimate of $4.05 for 2028, Tesla still trades at 103 times its projected earnings two years from now.

It's easy to say that Tesla stock is already priced to perfection and that buying shares today requires paying a premium. But a contrarian might say Tesla stock is undervalued because no company in history has ever successfully deployed physical AI at a global scale.

In other words, since there's nothing to benchmark Optimus against, it's impossible to know how big Tesla's robotics business could truly become.

Optimus remains an extremely asymmetric bet. If Tesla executes on this vision, the stock could soar to new highs rather easily. But if the product flops or struggles to scale, then Tesla stock could be due for a meaningful de-rating.

Against this backdrop, I would not buy Tesla stock today purely on the hype that Optimus will transform the company by the end of the year. In reality, it will take years -- or even decades -- before Optimus adds additional trillions of value to Tesla in the long run.

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $492,341 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $50,381 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $424,262 !*

Right now, we’re issuing “Double Down” alerts for three incredible companies , available when you join Stock Advisor , and there may not be another chance like this anytime soon.

See the 3 stocks »

*Stock Advisor returns as of February 23, 2026

Adam Spatacco has positions in Tesla. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Tesla. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

Tesla's Optimus Robot Could Reach Human-Level Proficiency in 2026 -- Time to Buy? was originally published by The Motley Fool
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla's (TSLA) humanoid robot, Optimus, is a key focus for its transformation into a technology-enabled services business.
• Production of Model S and X vehicles is being scaled down to repurpose the Fremont factory for Optimus manufacturing.
• Optimus Gen 3 has demonstrated fine motor skills and advanced dexterity in demonstrations.
• CEO Elon Musk aims to produce 1 million Optimus bots per year, but production is still in R&D and expected by year-end.
• The stock's valuation (202x forward P/E) is high, with Optimus's long-term impact uncertain but potentially significant.
news.Keywords: Humanoid Robot, AI Services, Manufacturing Pivot, R&D, Valuation

news.Title: 台股大漲927點收34700點　改寫多項新紀錄
news.Content:
（中央社記者曾仁凱台北24日電）不畏美國道瓊工業指數重跌821.91點，台股今天在台積電 (2330) 、台達電 (2308) 等權值股領軍下，逆勢開高走高，盤中大漲逾千點，最高衝上34786.42點。集中市場指數終場大漲927.56點，收在34700.82點，盤中與收盤指數雙創歷史新高，成交值新台幣8370.68億元。
台股漲勢銳不可擋，從農曆蛇年封關前到馬年開紅盤，集中市場指數已連續4個交易日打破歷史新高紀錄。
台股今天再度創下多項新紀錄，除了台股盤中及收盤指數雙創新高外，包括今天盤中最大漲點1013.16點、收盤漲點927.56點，皆為歷史第2大單日漲點，僅次於2025年4月10日盤中和收盤漲點的1632.71點及1608.27點。
此外，台股2月23日馬年紅盤日上市公司總市值達110兆841億元，創下歷史新高，今天再進一步創高至113兆992億元新紀錄。
根據台灣證券交易所統計，今天三大法人同步站在買方，合計買超836.85億元。其中，外資及陸資（不含外資自營商）今天買超627.33億元，為歷史第4大單日買超；投信、自營商今天分別買超56.03億元和153.49億元。
3大權值股今天股價全部收紅，權值雙雄台積電、台達電今天分別大漲65元及80元，攻上1965元及1385元歷史新天價，成為今天台股狂飆的最大功臣。
台積電今天終場大漲3.42%，收1965元，貢獻台股漲點約540點；台達電今天漲幅6.13%，收全日最高價1385元，貢獻台股漲點約68點；鴻海 (2317) 今天上漲3.5元或1.53%，收232元。
高價千金股方面，股王信驊 (5274) 今天震盪收平盤9755元；竑騰 (7751) 終場攻上漲停1005元，台股今天創下「34千金」新紀錄。觀察竹陞 (6739) 、漢唐 (2404) 、華城 (1519) 、高力 (8996) 、旭隼 (6409) 收盤價皆在900元以上，預期台股千金股陣容不排除可望進一步壯大。
AI效應外溢下，具缺貨漲價題材的PCB族群今天股價漲勢凶猛，南電 (8046) 、景碩 (3189) 、欣興 (3037) 、華通 (2313) 、燿華 (2367) 、榮科 (4989) 、泰鼎-KY (4927) 等今天收盤股價都攻上漲停板。
被動元件族群同樣表現強勁，國巨 (2327) 大漲19.5元或6.93%，收301元，飛越300元大關；華新科 (2492) 亮燈漲停攻上149元。（編輯：張良知）
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日收盤大漲927.56點，收在34700.82點，創下盤中與收盤指數雙歷史新高。
• 盤中最大漲點1013.16點、收盤漲點927.56點，皆為史上第2大單日漲點。
• 上市公司總市值創下113兆992億元新紀錄，台積電貢獻指數約540點。
• 三大法人同步買超，外資買超627.33億元，為歷史第4大單日買超。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 歷史新高, 漲點紀錄, 台積電, 外資買超

news.Title: 瘋馬行情3萬4再創高 台股飆927點史上第二大
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者林浩宇,攝影張皓普
台股再寫新高紀錄！儘管美股四大指數全數收黑，台股卻逆勢大漲，在台積電改寫新天價，帶動電子權值股與記憶體、ABF載板、矽光子等族群全面點火，盤中大漲千點，站穩34000點整數大關，加權指數終場上漲927點，創史上第二大漲點，以34700點作收，成交量8618億元，則創下史上第六大。外資周二大買627億元，創史上第四大買超紀錄，三大法人同站買方，單日買超836.5億元。
《非凡新聞》記者 林浩宇：「台股24日又再度寫下全新的一頁，盤中指數不斷的創下新高，不僅突破前高，更再次創下了歷史新高紀錄。」
原本市場預期，美股四大指數全數收黑，可能拖累台股表現，但結果跌破投資人眼鏡，加權指數開高走高，盤中大漲千點，一路朝35000點整數關卡邁進，對比去年全年漲點5928點，今年漲點短短不到兩個月時間，幾乎達到去年一整年的數字。
富邦投顧董事長 陳奕光：「這個漲法你會發現，你越往高點去挑戰，過程裡面天數越來越短，30000點差不多走了十一天，31000走了九天，32000走了六天，33000三天，34000一天就上去了，所以你會發現在整體行情的走法，是一個急牛行情。台股現在漲了20%，OTC漲13%，其實也才剛剛好，整個資金從歐美國家轉到亞洲國家，很明顯就是AI。」
護國神山台積電成為最大推手，與台達電雙雙寫下新天價紀錄，電子權值股全面點火，盤面更呈現百花齊放格局，記憶體族群力積電和旺宏攻上漲停，ABF載板三雄全數亮燈，矽光子與光通訊族群波若威及華星光也紅燈亮起，幾乎所有族群同步表態。
永誠投顧分析師 陳建誠：「這樣的一個成交量，還能夠支持股價持續往上，用類股輪動的方式，那有些族群就會持續再攻高，像ABF載板跟矽光子這一部分。其實許多因為這個漲價效應，而現在股價相對在底部的一些，成熟製程IC股，應該有機會隨著走出景氣谷底，價格也開始上漲的話，會帶動整個營收重返成長的軌道。」
從基本面觀察，動能同樣強勁，統計1月整體上市櫃合併營收高達4.83兆元，不僅表現亮眼，還同步創下3大里程碑。第一、連七月站穩4兆元大關紀錄之上，且年增率高達28.65%；第二、寫下「史上最旺1月」佳績；第三、創下單月營收歷史第二高紀錄。
而接下來市場的重頭戲，將是輝達財報在本周四公布，AI龍頭的成績單，不僅牽動台股後市動能，更成為下一波行情的關鍵風向球。（記者林浩宇、張皓普／台北報導）
觀看原文...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日盤中大漲逾千點，終場上漲927點，收在34700點，創史上第2大收盤漲點。
• 台積電創1975元新天價，帶動電子權值股與記憶體、ABF載板、矽光子等族群全面點火。
• 分析師認為，台股處於急牛行情，資金輪動快速，後續需留意成交量變化及獲利了結賣壓。
• 基本面方面，1月上市櫃合併營收創下歷史第二高紀錄，顯示經濟動能強勁。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 歷史新高, 台積電, AI概念股, 經濟動能

news.Title: 《台北股市》外資再砸627億元史上第4大 自營商轉買今年最大筆
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】金馬年台股無懼關稅雜音，24日盤中和收盤指數齊創高。護國神山引領，指數一度漲破千點，收盤飆漲927點，創歷史第二大漲點。
外資今加碼627億元，規模今年最大、史上第4大，連5買累計2039.25億元。投信和自營商齊轉向買方，其中自營商回補153億元，年內最大手筆。
今日外資(不含外資自營商)買超627.3355億元，投信買超56.0367億元，自營商買超153.4866億元，三大法人合計買超836.85億元。
加權指數盤中觸及34786.42點最高，收盤上漲927.56點或2.75%，報34700.82點，成交值放大至8370.67億元。台積電(2330)攻1975元新天價，終場上漲65元至1965元，貢獻大盤超過500點。
電子權值同步走強，聯電(2303)大漲5.63%，台達電(2308)勁揚6.13%，日月光投控(3711)更飆漲9.31%。載板三雄再噴，其中欣興(3037)鎖漲停價446元，全天成交值僅次台積電。
盤面資金持續挹注電子族群，記憶體、矽光子、PCB等表現亮眼。傳產方面以玻陶類股最為強勢，類股漲幅逼近9%，台玻(1802)受惠玻纖布題材再度亮燈，迄今連拉三根漲停板。
台股技術面維持多頭格局，不過短線快速逼近高檔壓力區，須留意追價力道是否持續，後續觀察電子權值與題材族群能否維持輪動上攻，將成為指數續創高的關鍵。(編輯：廖小蕎)
news.AI_Summary:
• 外資24日加碼627億元，規模今年最大、史上第4大，連5買累計2039.25億元。
• 自營商回補153億元，為年內最大手筆，投信也轉向買方，三大法人合計買超836.85億元。
• 台積電攻1975元新天價，終場上漲65元至1965元，貢獻大盤超過500點。
• 電子權值股同步走強，聯電、台達電、日月光投控表現亮眼，載板三雄欣興鎖漲停。
news.Keywords: 外資買超, 自營商買超, 台積電, 電子權值股, 載板三雄

外資今加碼627億元，規模今年最大、史上第4大，連5買累計2039.25億元。投信和自營商齊轉向買方，其中自營商回補153億元，年內最大手筆。
今日外資(不含外資自營商)買超627.3355億元，投信買超56.0367億元，自營商買超153.4866億元，三大法人合計買超836.85億元。
加權指數盤中觸及34786.42點最高，收盤上漲927.56點或2.75%，報34700.82點，成交值放大至8370.67億元。台積電(2330)攻1975元新天價，終場上漲65元至1965元，貢獻大盤超過500點。
電子權值同步走強，聯電(2303)大漲5.63%，台達電(2308)勁揚6.13%，日月光投控(3711)更飆漲9.31%。載板三雄再噴，其中欣興(3037)鎖漲停價446元，全天成交值僅次台積電。
盤面資金持續挹注電子族群，記憶體、矽光子、PCB等表現亮眼。傳產方面以玻陶類股最為強勢，類股漲幅逼近9%，台玻(1802)受惠玻纖布題材再度亮燈，迄今連拉三根漲停板。
台股技術面維持多頭格局，不過短線快速逼近高檔壓力區，須留意追價力道是否持續，後續觀察電子權值與題材族群能否維持輪動上攻，將成為指數續創高的關鍵。(編輯：廖小蕎)
news.AI_Summary:
• 外資24日加碼627億元，規模今年最大、史上第4大，連5買累計2039.25億元。
• 自營商回補153億元，為年內最大手筆，投信也轉向買方，三大法人合計買超836.85億元。
• 台積電攻1975元新天價，終場上漲65元至1965元，貢獻大盤超過500點。
• 電子權值股同步走強，聯電、台達電、日月光投控表現亮眼，載板三雄欣興鎖漲停。
news.Keywords: 外資買超, 自營商買超, 台積電, 電子權值股, 載板三雄

news.Title: 台股連4日創高　法人：台灣有關稅與產業2優勢
news.Content:
（中央社記者曾仁凱台北2026年02月24日電）週一美股主要指數全面下跌，道瓊工業指數重挫821.91點，今天台股卻開高走高，集中市場指數終場大漲927.56點，收在34700.82點，連續4個交易日改寫新高。對於台股持續創高表現，法人分析有2大原因。
第一金投顧研究部專業副總經理留政鈺分析，首先是關稅優勢，最近美國關稅又添變數，美國最高法院裁定總統川普去年實施廣泛的對等關稅無效，隨後川普依據貿易法第122條款，對全球各國徵收15%關稅。留政鈺指出，這個122條款關稅為期150天，為過渡性，重點應該放在後續的232和301條款。
留政鈺指出，台美政府農曆年前已完成關稅談判，台灣取得232條款的最優惠條件，而台灣最重要的半導體業已經在這個保護傘下，不受影響。即使是新的122條款，台灣的平均稅率比起韓國、日本、中國等主要競爭對手同樣具有優勢。
留政鈺認為，新的關稅變數下，國際資金找新的避風港，台灣除了具有稅率優勢，還有產業優勢。
留政鈺觀察，最近美股下跌很大一個原因，在於市場擔心AI對傳統軟體廠帶來顛覆的憂慮擴大，許多軟體股大跌，如老牌科技巨擘IBM昨天股價重挫13%，為造成道瓊指數大跌的重要禍首。
不過台灣科技業以硬體為主，目前AI仍處於資本支出持續擴大的階段，包括Google、微軟、亞馬遜、Meta等美國4大CSP（雲端服務供應商）最近紛紛上調今年資本支出，合計達到6600億美元，比去年再成長約6成，主要投入各種AI基建，台灣將成為主要受惠者，激勵台股。
留政鈺觀察，今年以來半導體類股股價走勢特別強，以美股為例，統計2026年初至2月23日收盤，標普500指數約下跌0.1%，那斯達克指數下跌2.6%，費城半導體指數卻大漲15.9%，而半導體產業正好是台灣的強項。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日連續4個交易日改寫新高，法人分析台灣在關稅及產業方面具有兩大優勢。
• 美國關稅變數下，台灣半導體業受惠於最優惠條件，平均稅率相較競爭對手具有優勢。
• AI資本支出持續擴大，台灣科技業以硬體為主，將成為主要受惠者，激勵台股表現。
• 法人看好台股企業獲利有望大幅上修，AI供應鏈仍是主流，建議聚焦先進製程、關鍵零組件及缺貨漲價方向。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 關稅優勢, 產業優勢, AI供應鏈, 獲利預估

news.Title: Should You Buy Apple Stock Before Its New Product Launch on March 4?
news.Content:
Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) is hosting a product launch on March 4. The new products are expected to include the new iPhone 17e, a lower-priced entry level iPhone that Bloomberg says will cost the same as last year's model -- starting at $599. New MacBook laptops and iPads might be coming soon too, but we don't know yet which items will be introduced at the March 4 event.

Let's look at a few reasons why an ambitious range of new product launches in 2026 could be a bullish signal for this tech stock .

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Ever since Sept. 9, 2025, when the iPhone 17 was announced, Apple's stock has gained about 13% and has outperformed the tech-heavy Nasdaq-100 index, which is up only 4.9% in that timeframe.

The new iPhone has been widely praised by reviewers. Anytime a new Apple product launch builds positive buzz and makes people want to stand in line at the mall to upgrade their phones, that's a good sign for the stock .

In January, the company reported record-breaking sales for its flagship smartphone, with a 23% year-over-year increase in net sales of the iPhone. The company's quarterly earnings report also showed double-digit year-over-year gains in diluted earnings per share (up 19%) and revenue (up 16%). Refreshing its product lineup could build upon Apple's positive momentum through the rest of 2026.

The company seems to be trying to win over customers at lower price points, which could help it address consumer concerns about affordability. One of the most anticipated new products that might be announced soon is a lineup of lower-cost MacBook laptops in a range of colors.

This could be a welcome move for budget-conscious customers who are cutting back on consumer discretionary spending. Apple is not known as a low-cost brand; it's built a reputation for premium pricing. But lower-cost products could give more customers an easier point of entry to the Apple ecosystem.

Lower-cost MacBooks could also help the company gain market share, especially with corporate customers who buy in bulk, and in emerging markets around the world. In its most recent quarterly sales results on Jan. 29, Apple reported big gains in the Greater China market -- with a 38% year-over-year increase in net sales. Greater China accounted for $25.5 billion of net sales in the latest quarter, about 18% of Apple's total net sales.

China is not the world's only emerging market, but it's one of the biggest and most important. Apple's recent strong growth there could be a positive sign for consumer demand for its new lower-cost MacBooks.

Details of the Apple product launch will be revealed soon. If the company can refresh its product lineup with a few new well-reviewed, lower-priced devices, this could help the stock recover from its sluggish start to 2026. Apple's stock is down 2.8% year to date, but now could be a good time for patient investors to buy the dip.
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple (AAPL) is launching new products on March 4, including a potential lower-priced iPhone 17e starting at $599.
• Apple's stock has gained ~13% since the iPhone 17 announcement, outperforming the Nasdaq-100.
• iPhone net sales increased 23% YoY in the latest quarter, with diluted EPS and revenue up double digits.
• Lower-cost MacBook laptops are anticipated, potentially attracting budget-conscious customers and corporate buyers.
• Strong growth in Greater China (38% YoY increase in net sales) suggests positive consumer demand for new products.
news.Keywords: Product Launch, Smartphone Market, Consumer Electronics, Market Share Expansion, Emerging Markets

news.Title: What Are the 3 Top Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks to Buy Right Now?
news.Content:
Artificial intelligence (AI) stocks have been soaring for most of the past few years amid optimism about the technology's game-changing potential. AI has already demonstrated some of its strengths, making companies more efficient, for example. In the future, it may continue to do this and more, reshaping the way the world operates. Importantly, this could lower companies' costs and boost innovation.

As a result, earnings and stock performance may take off -- and early investors in AI companies might score a significant win. But, with all of the AI stocks out there today, which ones should an investor choose? Here are the top 3 to buy right now.

Where to invest $1,000 right now? Our analyst team just revealed what they believe are the 10 best stocks to buy right now, when you join Stock Advisor. See the stocks »

Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) may be the world's most well-known AI company . That's because it's built an AI empire, selling everything -- from chips to networking tools -- a customer may need along the AI path. Its expertise in designing AI chips, or graphics processing units (GPUs), got the ball rolling and paved the way for Nvidia to construct this complete portfolio of products.

All of this has helped the chip leader to generate explosive revenue growth -- in the double and triple digits -- and at a high level of profitability on sales. In the latest quarter, revenue soared 62% to $57 billion, and gross margin topped 70%.

Nvidia's commitment to innovation should keep this leadership going, and with an AI market forecast to reach into the trillions by 2030, this company could have plenty of bright days ahead. That's why right now, with Nvidia trading at its lowest valuation in nearly a year, it's a great time to get in on this AI powerhouse.

Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) is a fantastic AI buy because it offers you exposure to a company well-positioned to benefit from AI -- and it already is doing so -- and a tech leader that doesn't rely entirely on this new technology. After all, it's important to remember that Amazon's e-commerce and cloud computing businesses built a track record of earnings growth well before the AI boom gathered momentum.

Meanwhile, Amazon has been growing its presence in AI as a user and a provider of the technology. For example, Amazon uses AI to choose the best delivery routes for packages, saving money and time. And Amazon Web Services (AWS), the company's cloud business, offers customers a wide range of AI products and services, from chips to a fully managed system called Amazon Bedrock.

All of this makes Amazon a great selection for investors looking for both safety and AI growth. Today, Amazon trades for 27x forward earnings estimates, down from more than 35x just a few months ago -- a very reasonable price for this top tech player.

Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) chief Mark Zuckerberg hasn't been shy about expressing his AI ambitions. He put a focus on AI a few years ago, launching the construction of Meta's own large language models, and has built on this effort. Today, Meta has its own superintelligence lab, continues to invest heavily in AI, and aims to become one of the industry's leaders.

How can Meta benefit from this technology? Meta relies on advertising for its billion-dollar revenue, and the company's AI strengths may prompt advertisers to spend more and more of their ad budgets on Meta. This is because AI features may keep us, advertisers' target audience, on Meta's social media apps longer. And Meta is working on AI to improve and automate the advertising process to make it more efficient and more effective.

On top of this, Meta's interest in AI could result in other products and services down the road.

Importantly, Meta has the financial resources to follow its ambitions and, at the same time, pay shareholders a dividend. All of this makes the stock look cheap at 21x forward earnings estimates.
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) is a leading AI company providing chips, networking tools, and a comprehensive product portfolio.
• Revenue soared 62% to $57 billion in the latest quarter, with gross margin topping 70%.
• Amazon (AMZN) offers AI exposure through its e-commerce, cloud (AWS), and AI services, with a forward P/E of 27x.
• Meta Platforms (META) is investing heavily in AI, aiming for industry leadership and leveraging AI to enhance its advertising business; trades at 21x forward earnings.
• Nvidia is trading at its lowest valuation in nearly a year, presenting a buying opportunity.
news.Keywords: AI Semiconductors, Cloud Services, Advertising Technology, Valuation, Market Leadership

news.Title: 〈台股盤後〉兩大權王雙駕馬車狂奔 創927點第2大漲點收34700點新高
news.Content:
台股今 (24) 日不受美股走弱影響，在台積電、台達電雙雙創高下，加權指數大漲 927.56 點或 2.75%，漲點創歷史第二大，收 34700.82 點也為歷史收盤高，成交量 8371 億元，總市值約 113.11 兆元。台股連續 5 個交易日大漲，累計漲幅達 2917.9 點。
台積電 (2330-TW) 盤中衝上 1975 元歷史新天價，收盤則上漲 65 元或 3.4%，收 1965 元；台達電 (2308-TW) 收在歷史新高價 1385 元，上漲 80 元或 6%，日月光投控 (3711-TW) 漲逾 9%，鴻海 (2317-TW)、聯發科 (2354-TW) 也雙雙收漲。
而昨日強勢族群今日表現普遍續強，力積電 (6770-TW)、旺宏 (2337-TW) 雙雙漲停，華邦電 (2344-TW) 漲 6%、南亞科 (2408-TW) 漲 3%；被動元件方面，國巨 *(2327-TW) 漲近 7%，重返 300 元之上，華新科 (2492-TW) 拉出第二根漲停。
ABF 載板三雄同樣延續昨日強勢，欣興 (3037-TW)、南電 (8046-TW)、景碩 (3189-TW) 全收漲停。PCB 相關的華通 (2313-TW)、聯茂 (6213-TW)、燿華 (2367-TW)、尖點 (8021-TW)、金居 (8358-TW)、台玻 (1802-TW) 等也漲停。
不過，健策 (3653-TW) 下跌 4%、勤誠 (8210-TW) 也跌 2%，在今日電子股中表現相對弱勢，另外，金融族群今日回檔，國泰金 (2882-TW)、富邦金 (2881-TW) 都跌逾 1%，傳產的華航 (2610-TW)、統一 (1216-TW)、儒鴻 (1476-TW) 也收跌。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日收盤飆漲927點，創史上第2大漲點，收在34700點，創歷史收盤新高。
• 台積電盤中衝上1975元新天價，終場收在1965元，市值飆升至51.2兆元。
• 電子權值股如台達電、日月光投控、鴻海、聯發科等均上漲，帶動指數走高。
• 記憶體、PCB、玻纖布等題材股表現強勁，力積電、旺宏漲停，華通、欣興、南電、景碩等也亮燈漲停。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 歷史新高, 台積電, AI概念股, 記憶體

• 電子權值股如台達電、日月光投控、鴻海、聯發科等均上漲，帶動指數走高。

news.Title: Should You Buy Vistra Stock While It's Below $200 -- Or Wait for a Better Yield?
news.Content:
Artificial intelligence (AI) has energy trading at a premium. The data centers that AI programs run on are gluttonously gobbling up every spare electron on the grid.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

The energy demand from data centers caused Virginia, which has more data centers in operation and under construction than any other state, to become the largest energy importing state in 2023.

What's particularly impressive about that is the fact that Virginia has a population of about 9 million, and the state it replaced as the largest energy importer was California, which has a population of about 40 million. So, the increase is primarily due to the data centers.

And that has been the result in just one state. Data centers are driving huge demand for electricity, and companies like Vistra Corp (NYSE: VST) have been making money hand over fist meeting that need. Vistra alone is up 652% over the past three years.

However, Vistra dipped through the end of 2025 and is now back below $200, which makes its dividend yield look much better. So, should you pick up shares of Vistra now? Or should you wait and see?

Let's find out.

Vistra is based in Irving, Texas, but it provides electricity across the country. Its assets, which collectively generate about 44,000 megawatts, include both fossil fuel plants and green energy like nuclear and solar. The company operates 50 renewable plants nationwide.

And the company is focused on the AI market. In January of this year, it spent $4 billion on 10 natural gas power plants across the Northeast U.S. and Texas expressly for the purpose of powering data centers. That follows the $6.8 billion acquisition of its current nuclear fleet in 2024 and a $1.9 billion purchase of seven gas plants in May 2024.

The buying spree during Vistra's most recently reported quarter (Q3 2025) appears to be paying off. Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) for the first nine months of 2025 totaled $4.17 billion, up 13.9% over the first nine months of 2024.

That same quarter, the company managed an EBITDA margin of 29.9% and a net profit margin of 6.99%. Vistra channels those profits into a dividend which it has paid and raised every year since 2019.

Now, prior to 2024 and the bulk of Vistra's bull run, that dividend yielded a solid 2% to 3%, but the company's considerable share-price growth has lowered the company's dividend yield to 0.52%.

Despite the low current yield, there's a case to be made for Vistra. The company's shares are up about 6% year to date, and it is beginning to wipe out its decline at the end of 2025. If that continues, the yield will get lower.

However, Vistra is growing its dividend at a nice clip. The five-year compound annual growth rate (CAGR) for its dividend is 10.7% at present. And with a current payout ratio of 32.2%, Vistra has plenty of runway to continue growing its dividend.

With a yield unlikely to get much better than it is now, barring a catastrophic crash in Vistra's share price, this is one stock to consider for your energy-dividend portfolio -- and one you will likely want to watch for further dips in share price to see if you can increase your yield.
news.AI_Summary:
• Data centers for AI are driving significant energy demand, benefiting energy companies like Vistra Corp (VST).
• Vistra has seen a 652% stock increase over the past three years and recently acquired 10 natural gas power plants for data centers.
• Adjusted EBITDA for the first nine months of 2025 totaled $4.17 billion, up 13.9% YoY.
• EBITDA margin was 29.9% and net profit margin was 6.99% in Q3 2025.
• The company has a 10.7% dividend CAGR and a 32.2% payout ratio, offering potential for dividend growth.
news.Keywords: Energy Demand, Data Centers, AI Infrastructure, EBITDA Growth, Dividend Growth

news.Title: What TD Cowen’s $60 EPS Call Means for Micron Technology (MU)
news.Content:
Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MU) is among the 15 Undervalued Momentum Stocks That Are Taking Off . According to a February 9 report, TD Cowen raised its long-term earnings outlook for Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ: MU ). The firm now expects that MU’s EPS may reach as much as $60 in 2026, above its prior bull-case assumption of $50. The revision reflects the firm’s view that structural supply capacity issues in the DRAM market are likely to persist for multiple years. This implies that the company may continue to witness stronger pricing and margin development, better than previously anticipated. TD Cowen outlines a “gradual re-rating path” toward a 12x multiple applied to a normalized earnings base of roughly $50 per share, which underpins its longer-term upside thesis. According to the firm’s estimates, better-than-expected earnings growth could lead to a stock price of $600 over time.

Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MU) received a further boost when its management strongly backed its HBM4 product at Wolfe Research’s Autos/Semis conference on February 11. Notably, the leadership said they expect to start shipping the product in the current quarter rather than next, as previously guided. They also mentioned that demand is “significantly higher” than its supply capacity, thus underpinning demand trends beyond 2026. The performance of Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MU) shares has been stunning, with 240% returns in 2025 and 50% rally so far in 2026. The stock currently trades 6% below its 52-week high of $455.5. Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MU) designs, develops, manufactures, and markets memory and storage products, including dynamic random-access memory (DRAM), flash memory (NAND), solid-state drives (SSDs), and High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) globally. While we acknowledge the potential of MU as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you’re looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock .
news.AI_Summary:
• TD Cowen raised its long-term EPS outlook for Micron Technology (MU) to $60 in 2026, up from $50.
• This revision reflects expectations of persistent structural supply capacity issues in the DRAM market, leading to stronger pricing and margins.
• The firm outlines a 'gradual re-rating path' to a 12x multiple on normalized earnings, potentially leading to a $600 stock price.
• Micron's management strongly backs its HBM4 product, expecting to ship this quarter with demand significantly higher than supply.
• The stock has seen 240% returns in 2025 and 50% in 2026 so far, trading near its 52-week high.
news.Keywords: DRAM Market, EPS Outlook, HBM4, Pricing Power, Supply Constraints

news.Title: 《盤後解析》權王刷新天價1975元 台股創雙高收34700點
news.Content:
【時報記者張佳琪台北報導】新春紅包行情旺，台股今（24）日由台積電(2330)攻新天價1975元帶領電子人氣股群起上攻，指數盤中漲千點，最高至34786點再寫新高。終場大盤勁揚927點，以34700點作收，收盤價亦為新高，成交量8371億元。
美股主要指數全部收低，台股不甩美股走勢，開高強漲迅速衝破3萬4千點大關，且在日股、陸股揚升，以及美期貨電子盤帶動下，盤勢漲幅持續擴大。台積電ADR雖跌，現貨股價走自己的路，盤中漲幅逾3%，最高至1975元，一檔個股貢獻指數約500點，市值一天內暴增逾1.5兆，收盤更以1965元上漲65元作收。
記憶體族群人氣紅不讓，尤其旺宏(2337)開低後迅速翻紅，且在買單追逐下一路走高攻上漲停板113.5元鎖住並創新高，最受到矚目。華邦電(2344)、南亞科(2408)也連袂帶量上攻，分別出現6%、3%漲幅。
被動元件龍頭國巨*(2327)接連2天大漲站回300元大關，帶動華新科(2492)再拉一根漲停成為馬年飆股。PCB族群不遑多讓，燿華(2367)、榮科(4989)、泰鼎-KY(4927)盤中都攻上漲停板，華通(2313)、臻鼎-KY(4958)人氣旺。載板三雄欣興(3037)、南電(8046)、景碩(3189)高姿態，連袂漲停鎖住作收；欣興更成為台股市場成交金額第二大個股，僅次於台積電。
台達電(2308)漲逾6%，股價最高漲至1385元，雖被證交所列入注意股，無礙於漲勢，加上日月光投控(3711)大漲逾9%，聯電(2303)盤中漲約6%，以及面板二虎同步走高，台光電(2383)、鴻海(2317)、聯發科(2454)均上漲，推升指數盤中漲千點。
儘管金融與航空股扯後腿，指數仍強漲927點收市。資深分析師簡伯儀表示，大盤再創新高，整體的多頭氣氛延續，目前日K線連續6天收紅，股價也維持在所有均線之上，搭配題材股持續維持強勢，台股後續應可維持偏多操作。由於OTC的指數同步大漲2.5%，簡伯儀認為，目前市場類股輪流強勢，操作上強勢股應仍有買盤逢低買進。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日由台積電(2330)攻新天價1975元帶領，指數盤中漲千點，最高至34786點再寫新高。
• 終場大盤勁揚927點，以34700點作收，收盤價亦為新高，成交量8371億元。
• 記憶體族群人氣紅不讓，旺宏、華邦電、南亞科表現強勢，被動元件龍頭國巨帶動華新科漲停。
• PCB族群、載板三雄欣興、南電、景碩連袂漲停，台達電、日月光投控、聯電等電子權值股也上漲。
news.Keywords: 台積電, 股價創新高, 記憶體, 被動元件, PCB載板

news.Title: 台股再衝歷史新高！霸榜前十名「吸金股」出爐 全是漲價概念股的天下
news.Content:
台股再衝歷史新高，今日再度以34,700點見證歷史，漲價概念股組成的多頭大軍戴著鋼盔往前衝居功厥偉，拜漲聲不斷的記憶體和玻纖布及近期喊漲的面板股吸金力道大增，前十大成交量股漲價概念股成功霸屏八成，其中面板雙虎和玻纖布三雄更是全數進榜。
漲價概念股續發威，且威力久久不散，火力一路由玻纖布、記憶體延燒到面板，儼然成為台股明牌新指標，在買盤爭相湧入下，今日台股前十大成交量股也幾近由漲價概念股霸屏，依今日成交量排序，依序為群創、力積電、友達、華邦電、台玻、聯電、南亞、富喬、旺宏、華通，除已蟬連多時的記憶體族群成員含力積電、華邦電、旺宏，及玻纖布三雄台玻、南亞、富喬外，昨日初聞漲聲的面板股也現身爭光，繼群創搶下今日成交量冠軍寶座，友達也尾隨拿下季軍。
根據研調機構TrendForce 23日新公布的2026年2月下旬面板價格預測，受惠於面板廠春節期間執行五至七天不等的產能調節、品牌端急單湧現，電視與顯示器面板價格漲勢確立，市場推測即使春節連假結束，買賣雙方在價格議定上變化也不大，電視面板價格仍將維持上漲態勢，預估32吋、43吋與50吋上漲1美元，55吋上漲2美元，65吋因需求較好，上漲3美元，75吋上漲2美元。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日再衝歷史新高，漲價概念股表現強勁，記憶體、玻纖布及面板股吸金力道強。
• 前十大成交量股中，漲價概念股佔八成，面板雙虎及玻纖布三雄全數進榜。
• 面板價格預測顯示，受惠於產能調節及急單湧現，電視與顯示器面板價格漲勢確立。
• 記憶體、玻纖布及面板族群成為台股新指標，群創奪成交量冠軍，友達緊隨其後。
news.Keywords: 漲價概念股, 記憶體, 面板, 玻纖布, 成交量

news.Title: 狂飆近千點！台股刷新「史上第二大漲點」　熱錢湧入記憶體、PCB 「這3檔」飆股出線
news.Content:
[ FTNN新聞網 ]記者蕭廷芬／綜合報導
台股今（24）開盤後一路震盪走高，盤中一度狂飆逾千點。終場加權指數大漲927.56點，收在34,700.82點，不僅成交量爆出8,370億元的驚人天量，盤中與收盤漲點更雙雙締造「台股史上第二大」的歷史紀錄！在權王台積電（2330）強攻領軍下，終場欣興（3037）上漲40.5元、華通（2313）上漲20.5元、力積電（6770）上漲6.7元，表現吸睛。
權值股方面，台積電（2330）無疑是今日指數強攻的最關鍵推手，收盤上漲65元至1,965元，漲幅3.42%，成交量突破4萬張；股價盤中最高來到1,975元，距離2,000元整數大關僅一步之遙。其他權值大軍亦同步回神，鴻海（2317）上漲3.5元至232元；聯發科（2454）大漲30元至1,825元；廣達（2382）由黑翻紅小漲0.5元收284元。
觀看今天其他飆股，火力集中PCB載板與記憶體，力積電（6770）：爆出32萬張天量漲停，收在73.7元，大漲6.7元亮燈漲停，成交量32萬5,640張。華通（2313）收在225.5元，上漲20.5元，尾盤牢牢鎖在漲停價，成交量達113,429張。欣興（3037）收在446元，上漲40.5元，成交量84,477張，盤中攻上漲停並鎖死至收盤。
◎《FTNN新聞網》提醒您：本資料僅供參考，投資人應獨立判斷，審慎評估並自負投資風險。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日飆漲927.56點，收在34,700.82點，創下史上第二大漲點紀錄，成交量爆出8,370億元天量。
• 台積電（2330）收盤上漲65元至1,965元，盤中最高來到1,975元，距離2,000元關卡僅一步之遙。
• 火力集中PCB載板與記憶體族群，力積電、華通、欣興表現吸睛，力積電更爆量漲停。
• 權值股如鴻海、聯發科、廣達、台達電、日月光投控等亦同步回神，帶動整體市場上漲。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 歷史紀錄, 台積電, PCB載板, 記憶體

news.Title: 台積領軍，矽光子、ABF、被動元件、記憶體群起攻堅，台股創新高
news.Content:
【財訊快報／方亞申】關稅議題壓抑市場氣氛，美國總統川普週一再度強調其提高關稅的能力，並警告若有國家「耍花樣」將面臨更高關稅。此外，市場再度同時受到人工智慧(AI)衝擊疑慮，軟體再受重創，美股週一道瓊指數狂瀉逾800點，標普500指數下跌1%，那斯達克綜合指數下跌1.1%。科技股除了光纖、輝達、半導體設備、零售、能源外，普遍下跌；週二亞洲股市卻又在韓國記憶體、日本電子原料、半導體相關股拉抬下，除香港外大都上漲，台股在ABF、玻纖布、被動元件、記憶體、衛星以及台積電(2330)、日月光(3711)、台達電(2308)、台塑集團股拉抬，終場大漲927.56點，收在34700.82點，成交量8370.67億元。美國最高法院上週否決其「對等關稅」後，川普已於上週六表示，將全球關稅自10%提高至15%，並稱新關稅將立即生效，但相關正式文件時程仍不明確，未來數月也可能推出更多關稅措施。歐洲官員對此表達關切，認為此舉可能衝擊美歐貿易協議。歐洲議會週一宣布，已暫停批准美歐貿易協議的相關作業。一般認為若按照最高法院否決對等關稅，中、印、巴西將是大贏家，但是歐、日、英國將是輸家，台灣、韓國則限於尷尬局面，而美國內部爭議不斷，全球經濟又限於不穩定局面。主要還是以課徵關稅要不要返還給已課徵國家，要花數千億美元去美國投資國家是否持續？不過以川普政府強硬態度，可能會另尋他法，美國國內協商恐怕有得拖一陣子。再者，美國及伊朗在波斯灣對峙，開戰與否仍是未定之天，國際油價今年以來已上漲超過一成，若進而引發通膨美國降息是否能按原來順序？後續變數多。
重要是AI的加速興起以及CSP大廠鉅額投資，尚未獲得實質巨大收益，卻已出現裁員聲浪不斷，近期軟體股跌跌不休，就是AI可取代人工編寫軟體，IBM股價重挫超過10%。近一個月科技巨頭公司幾乎每家都又投下數千億美元投入AI基礎建設或是買晶片，股價跌多漲少，接下來就是週三晚間輝達財報公布定生死。
技術上道瓊回測季線、標普及那指跌破季線，都有拉長整理時間。費半雖還在月線之上，主要是記憶體、設備支撐，一旦輝達再下去則費半指數也要向季線測試支撐。其實美股回檔也算是自去年4月中上旬大漲以來技術性拉回。
亞洲的日韓股市由於有記憶體、玻纖布、PCB鑽頭、半導體設備、被動元件等AI原物料及零組件，表現較強，韓股向6000點，日股也向新高挑戰，表現最強。中國股市反彈，倒是港股偏弱勢。國際黃金、白銀、油價都再度反彈，令人不得不注意。
台股週一以電子次族群記憶體、ABF、玻纖布、矽光子相關族群表現最強，南亞(1303)及金融股也出現少見強勢，拉高指數，雖留上影線，融資也增加47億元，不過週二ABF四家除臻鼎(4958)外，南電(8046)量縮漲停，其次景碩(3189)、欣興(3037)也跟上，外資高喊超級循環，要到2028年才能滿足供給，是台股最強電子次族群。其次美國及日本光纖股諸如LITE、康寧、古河電工等表現後來居上強勢，使得台股相關的波若威(3163)、華星光(4979)、光聖(6442)、上詮(3363)、統新(6426)、聯亞(3081)表現都很強；而日東紡織、村田、太陽誘電更強，刺激南亞、台玻(1802)、華新科(2492)、國巨(2327)、禾伸堂(3026)、日電貿(3090)、九豪(6127)、華容(5328)、三集瑞(6862)、凱美(2375)、臺慶科(3357)等全數大漲。玻纖布的台玻(1802)、富喬(1815)、建榮(5340)、德宏(5475)都大漲。
衛星相關股啟碁(6285)、華通(2313)、昇達科(3491)、萊德光電(7717)、耀登(3138)以及記憶體旺宏(2337)、力積電(6770)、群聯(8299)、南亞科(2408)等續漲，可沿均線操作。台積電大聯盟萬潤(6187)、達興材料(5234)、中砂(1560)、漢唐(2404)以及封測的日月光(3711)、京元電(2449)、欣銓(3264)、華東(8110)，乃至世界(5347)、聯電(2303)都很強。
傳產以南亞、台玻及華新(1605)為主，金融彰銀(2801)、凱基金(2883)、元大金(2885)表現較強，整體以進三退二為主。
台股指數再創歷史新高，台積電是中流砥柱，超級循環的記憶體、ABF、矽光子、衛星等次族群是強弱指標。台股以5日線為強弱關卡。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股在台積電領軍下，ABF、玻纖布、被動元件、記憶體等族群表現強勢，再創歷史新高。
• 美國關稅議題及AI衝擊疑慮未能影響台股多頭氣勢，亞洲股市普遍上漲。
• 記憶體、玻纖布、PCB鑽頭、半導體設備、被動元件等AI相關原物料及零組件表現較強。
• 台股指數再創歷史新高，台積電是中流砥柱，超級循環的記憶體、ABF、矽光子、衛星等次族群是強弱指標。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 歷史新高, 台積電, AI概念股, 記憶體

news.Title: Why Tesla Stock Could Double as Optimus Reaches Human-Level Proficiency This Year
news.Content:
Throughout the artificial intelligence (AI) revolution, Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) stock has experienced a number of pronounced peaks and valleys. Whether it's Elon Musk's foray into politics, an electric vehicle (EV) business in decline, or ongoing delays with the company's long-awaited robotaxi service, it's never boring over at Tesla.

Nevertheless, the stock has been resilient, and recent price action suggests investors are generally optimistic. One of the biggest catalysts fueling Tesla's enthusiasm is the company's humanoid robot, Optimus.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Let's dig into how Tesla plans to scale the Optimus business and assess how robotics could become the key to a compounding earnings profile in the long run.

At the end of January, Tesla reported earnings for the fourth quarter and full year 2025. Musk notified investors that the company plans to pivot some of its manufacturing capacity in Fremont, California, to wind down production of the Model S and X vehicles.

As production comes to an end for these EV models, Tesla will transition part of Fremont into a space focused on Optimus in lieu of the Model S and X.

You might not think that robotics offers robust profit margins, given that these devices require substantial capital outlays for research and development (R&D) and capital expenditures (capex).

One way Tesla has built a profitable business is by integrating high-margin services into its core hardware products. Take the EV business. Broadly speaking, buying a car is a one-time purchase -- or at the very least, a nonrecurring purchase.

Tesla's vehicle business is supplemented by a services platform featuring the company's autonomous driving technology . In other words, if you want access to Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) software, you need to sign up for a subscription. Wrapping a high-margin, recurring-revenue software service around the capital-intensive EV business has helped Tesla generate healthy gross margins and free cash flow.

Tesla will likely employ a similar playbook with Optimus. In addition to the one-time hardware sale of the robot itself, customers will pay a subscription fee alongside that allows Optimus to function, learn new tasks, and improve dexterity over time.

Humanoid robots have the potential to add trillions of dollars of economic value by delivering unprecedented levels of efficiency to the labor force. But Tesla is far from the only company exploring humanoids.

Moreover, Musk indicated that initial production of Optimus Gen 3 is likely to begin only at the end of this year. This is all to say that Optimus won't materially impact Tesla's financial profile for several years.

Here is where things get interesting: Wall Street forecasts Tesla's earnings to return to growth this year. By 2028, the company's earnings per share are expected to double. That may not seem like much, but the gap between Tesla's trailing-12-month EPS and its 2028 expected earnings implies significant growth ahead.

This profitability will not be driven solely by more EV sales. Rather, it appears that Wall Street anticipates Tesla successfully executing on its AI vision and beginning to scale Optimus in a material way over the next couple of years.

Should Tesla meet or exceed these expectations, the stock could be in a position to double as earnings growth compounds thanks to the company's dominance in the physical AI realm.

news.Title: 午盤飆漲逾千點！台股終場收34700點、上漲927點　台積電收1965元
news.Content:
即時中心／綜合報導
美股受到美國最高法院對關稅裁決影響，加上市場對人工智慧仍有疑慮，週一（23日）全數收跌，道瓊工業指數更大跌逾8百點！台股在農曆年後首個交易日收紅盤，今（24）日開盤率先突破3萬4大關，台積電盤中不僅衝出1,975元新天價，大盤更受激勵飆漲1013.16點、來到34,786.42點，續寫盤中歷史新高紀錄！終場指數就以34,700.82點作收、上漲927.56點，漲幅2.75％，成交量8,370.67億元。台積電（2330）終場以1,965元作收、上漲65元。
電子權值股方面，鴻海（2317）收在232元，漲3.5元；聯發科（2454）收1,825元，上漲30元；廣達（2382）收284元，小漲0.5元；台達電（2308）以1,385元作收，大漲80元；日月光投控（3711）收381.5元，上漲32.5元；聯電（2303）收67.5元，漲3.6元；大立光（3008）收在2,430元，漲60元。
金融方面，富邦金（2881）收94.1元，跌1.6元；國泰金（2882）收在78.2元，下滑1.4元；中信金（2891）以53.8元作收，跌0.1元；兆豐金（2886）收在40.8元，下跌0.15元；華南金（2880）收在37.35元，漲0.65元；第一金（2892）收30元，漲0.1元；合庫金（5880）以24.05元作收，下跌0.2元。
今日成交量前10名個股依序為群創、力積電、友達、華邦電、台玻、聯電、南亞、旺宏、華通、燿華。此外，漲幅前五名為華通、光罩、力積電、欣興、睿生光電；至於跌幅前五名則為永冠-KY、一詮、奧義賽博-KY創、東訊、瀚荃。
《民視新聞網》提醒您：內容僅供參考，投資人於決策時應審慎評估風險，並就投資結果自行負責。
原文出處： 快新聞／午盤飆漲逾千點！台股終場收34700點、上漲927點 台積電收1965元
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日盤中飆漲逾千點，終場上漲927.56點，收在34,700.82點，創下史上第二大單日漲點紀錄。
• 台積電（2330）盤中衝出1,975元新天價，終場以1,965元作收、上漲65元。
• 電子權值股如鴻海、聯發科、廣達、台達電、日月光投控等均上漲，帶動指數走高。
• 成交量前10名個股依序為群創、力積電、友達、華邦電、台玻、聯電、南亞、旺宏、華通、燿華。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 歷史新高, 台積電, 電子權值股, 成交量

news.Title: 史上第二猛！台股收盤暴漲927點 台積電收1965元天價
news.Content:
昨日道瓊重挫逾800點，美股四大指數面收黑，主因美國總統川普再度強調自己有能力提高全球關稅，不過台股今（24）日表現出奇強勁，最高暴漲逾千點，終場漲927.56點，收在34700.82點，漲幅2.75%，成交量約8371億，創下史上第2大收盤漲點。
台積電再飆新天價，最高攻上1975元，終場收在1965元。權值股紛紛上攻，台達電大漲80元收1385元新天價，鴻海漲逾1.5%，加上記憶體、PCB、玻纖布等題材股多檔走強上攻。分析師林漢偉提醒，若台股成交量失控放大，就要留意高檔震盪加劇風險。
回顧台股史上收盤最大漲點，第1名在2025年4月10日，當天加權指數大漲1608.27點，收19,000.03點，漲幅9.25%，被視為台股史上最大單日漲點與罕見「幾乎全面漲停」的一天。
台股23日收31961.51點，漲215.43點或0.68%，總成交值8154.21億元。三大法人買超271.50億元，其中外資買超232.09億元，投信、自營商分別賣超7.46億元及買超46.87億元。
美股道瓊下跌821.91點或1.66%，收48,804.06點。S&P500下跌71.76點或1.04%，收6,837.75點。NASDAQ下跌258.80點或1.13%，收22,627.27點。費城半導體下跌46.96點或0.57%，收8,213.46點。

• 權值股紛紛上攻，台達電大漲80元收1385元新天價，鴻海漲逾1.5%，加上記憶體、PCB、玻纖布等題材股多檔走強。
• 分析師提醒，若台股成交量失控放大，就要留意高檔震盪加劇風險。

news.Content:
[ FTNN新聞網 ]財經中心/綜合報導
台股氣勢如虹頻創新高。也讓去年甫問世的主動式台股ETF持續火熱，不僅檔數來到11檔，受益人數更是連續18周創新高。根據集保戶股權分散2月11日最新統計資料顯示，主動式台股ETF受益人數上周增加7731人，總人數來到835804人，統計今年來已增加203419人。其中包括主動群益台灣強棒(00982A)、主動統一台股增長(00981A)、主動復華未來50(00991A)、主動野村臺灣優選(00980A)、主動第一金台股優(00994A)五檔受益人數同步創下新高。據了解，主動式台股ETF配息王主動群益台灣強棒(00982A) 本次預估年化配息率均超過10%，配息金額都創新高，今年來績效表現也都不錯，欲參與除息的投資人最後買進日在2月25日。
群益投信台股ETF研究團隊表示，台股主動式ETF儼然已成為主流投資趨勢由於主動式操作上，可適時參酌時事對各產業的影響分析，每日即時調整投資組合，彰顯出因應市場而主動靈活選股策略的優勢。投資人可多利用台股主動式ETF，參與台股投資契機。特別是具有高息的主動式台股ETF可提供股息，又有潛在的價差空間可期，是當前市場變化快速的環境下，可多加善用的投資台股工具。
主動群益台灣強棒ETF (00982A)經理人陳沅易表示，AI帶動的科技創新仍在起步階段；Google & Meta今年CAPEX接近2025年的兩倍，Amazon在高基期下投資支出也成長近6成，顯示AI軍備競賽仍在延續。AI一定還是主旋律，但是賺錢的效應會逐漸擴散到其他行業；資金已經從AI製造相關供應鏈，開始轉向關鍵零組件，包括記憶體、PCB、測試、光通訊等行業。2026年政策的負面干擾將會降低，美國今年的期中選舉以及台灣九合一選舉皆在11月舉行，預期政策作多方向不變；今年台股仍是全球投資人資產配置的首選之一。
◎《FTNN新聞網》提醒您：本資料僅供參考，投資人應獨立判斷，審慎評估並自負投資風險。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股氣勢如虹頻創新高，去年問世的主動式台股ETF持續火熱，受益人數連續18周創新高。
• 主動式台股ETF受益人數今年來已增加203419人，其中群益台灣強棒(00982A)等五檔受益人數創高。
• 主動式台股ETF配息王主動群益台灣強棒(00982A)年化配息率均超過10%，今年來績效表現不錯。
• 法人認為AI帶動的科技創新仍在起步階段，資金已從AI製造供應鏈轉向關鍵零組件，看好台股多頭趨勢。
news.Keywords: 主動式ETF, 台股基金, 配息王, AI趨勢, 資金輪動

news.Title: 台積電2000元快來了！近18萬股東聽魏哲家的話 一張最高賺102萬元
news.Content: 台積電（2330）今（24）日盤中來到1,975元歷史天價，回顧去年6月，董事長魏哲家曾公開信心喊話「多買台積電股票就不會錯」，如今印證其預言，若當時持有一張不賣到今天，獲利最高達102.5萬元，報酬率翻2倍。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台積電24日盤中來到1,975元歷史天價，回顧去年6月，董事長魏哲家曾喊話「多買台積電股票就不會錯」。
• 若當時持有台積電一張股票至今未賣，獲利最高可達102.5萬元，報酬率翻2倍。
• 此現象展現了長期持有優質科技股的價值，也反映了市場對台積電的信心。
• 台積電股價持續創高，顯示其在AI時代的關鍵地位。
news.Keywords: 台積電, 股價天價, 股東獲利, 魏哲家, AI時代

news.Title: Better Artificial Intelligence Stock: Navitas vs. Arm
news.Content:
Artificial intelligence (AI) has fueled the returns of many technology stocks in recent years and, in many cases, has caused companies to shift their focus to AI.

That is the case with two AI stocks , Navitas Semiconductor (NASDAQ: NVTS) and Arm Holdings (NASDAQ: ARM) . These technology companies have moved aggressively into AI infrastructure and captured the attention of investors.

Where to invest $1,000 right now? Our analyst team just revealed what they believe are the 10 best stocks to buy right now, when you join Stock Advisor. See the stocks »

Navitas, a chipmaker, has seen its stock price surge 188% over the past 12 months.

Chip designer Arm's stock price is up 13% year to date, but down 21% over the past year. But Arm stands out as the better buy right now. Here's why.

Navitas makes gallium nitride chips, which are used in smartphones and consumer electronics. These types of chips are considered more powerful and efficient than traditional silicon chips in those devices. Navitas is one of a handful of leaders in this space.

However, it is in the process of pivoting toward providing chips for larger power markets like AI data centers, electric vehicles, and certain industrial markets. Leadership calls the pivot Navitas 2.0, as Chris Allexandre, president and CEO, explained last November:

Revenue will take a hit, as the firm anticipates just $7 million in revenue in Q4 2025 during this pivot. That's down from $10 million in Q3 and $22 million in the third quarter of 2024. For the year, analysts anticipate just $36 million in revenue, down from $45 million last fiscal year.

But Navitas is confident that with its partnerships with hyperscalers like Nvidia , its strong brand and technology, and the expected boom in AI factories, it can accelerate revenue generation over time. Analysts project about $130 million in revenue and $18 million in earnings by 2028.

It is a bold pivot by Navitas, but analysts are pretty mixed on it. While the AI data center market has massive and growing demand, there are many larger competitors that it must contend with. It is indeed a gamble that is not expected to pay off until 2027 or 2028, if revenue projections are correct.

Arm is better positioned for both the short term and the long term. The UK-based company designs CPU chips and licenses the technology to other companies so they can build their chips with Arm's designs.

It is a dominant player in designing CPUs for mobile phones, with its CPU technology in about 99% of smartphones. But in recent years, it has begun to more aggressively expand its CPU designs to handle AI workloads as part of its AI Everywhere strategy.

At the end of 2025, more than half of Arm's revenue was coming from nonmobile licenses, driven by AI data centers.

On the fiscal year Q3 2026 (ended Dec. 31, 2025) earnings call, CEO Rene Anthony Andrada Haas said data center royalties grew 100% year over year. In addition, its market share among hyperscalers for AI data center CPUs is about 50%. That's up from around 18% in 2024.

"We expect in a few years our data center business to be our largest business, larger than mobile," the CEO said.

In fiscal Q4 2026 (ending March 31, 2026), Arm projects an 18% revenue increase over Q3. Looking out, some analysts project 50% compound annual growth for this segment through 2030.

Navitas may get there someday in its pivot, but Arm is already there in its markets. It remains the dominant force in smartphone and consumer electronics CPU designs and is seeing incredible growth in data centers that is only accelerating.

Wall Street analysts rate Arm as a consensus buy with a $147.50 median price target, which would represent 17% growth.

The big concern for Arm is its valuation, which is sky high and is why the stock price has come down about 21%. Looking out, it could settle even lower, as investors take profits and sell high. While Arm is the better long-term play, look for an opportunity to get in at a lower valuation.
news.AI_Summary:
• Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) is pivoting to larger power markets like AI data centers and EVs, projecting $130 million revenue by 2028.
• Arm Holdings (ARM) designs CPU chips and licenses technology, with over half its revenue from non-mobile licenses driven by AI data centers.
• Arm's data center royalties grew 100% YoY, with a 50% market share among hyperscalers for AI data center CPUs.
• Arm projects 50% compound annual growth for its data center business through 2030, expecting it to become its largest segment.
• Arm's valuation is considered high, with a consensus 'buy' rating and a median price target of $147.50.
news.Keywords: Semiconductor Design, AI Data Centers, Market Share, Revenue Growth, Valuation

news.Title: 台股馬年續創佳績 00735獨吃台韓半導體記憶體大廠 近年飆漲9成
news.Content:
台股馬年開盤即展現強勢上漲力道，台積電盤中創1,935元新天價，另一國際股市韓股則創新高點，全台多檔ETF買氣熱絡，觀察2月23日單日ETF成交量，包括00878、00735及00909等交易量都明顯升溫，其中高股息人氣王00878就有超過12萬張的成交量，國泰投信指出，00878適逢本周四除息，因具備歷年多次的填息紀錄，有現金流需求的存股族們持續趕在明天（25日）的最後買進日前積極加碼。
另一檔買氣增溫的00735則是國泰投信旗下的海外科技型ETF「國泰臺韓科技（00735）」，在最新出爐的投信投顧公會基金績效排名中，近1年報酬高達90.38％（2026年1月），勇奪績效冠軍，在韓國科技股持續噴發的態勢下，00735人氣跟買氣也直線上升，讓許多投資人直呼相見恨晚。
國泰投信ETF研究團隊分析這波韓國科技股再次在國際發光，最大原因就是記憶體的海量需求，今年以來記憶體結構性缺貨狀況仍未見改善可能，加上AI推論需求大幅攀升，HBM需求同步爆量，除了美系廠商美光以外，韓國三星和SK海力士受惠最大，可以看到韓國科技巨頭三星電子更領先同業，成為全球首家開始量產第六代高頻寬記憶體（HBM4）的記憶體巨頭，甚至搭上輝達最新一代的Rubin架構，未來量產後對三星公司的業績成長更具想像空間。
市場分析師則認為三星股價還有上行空間，預估今年第一季EPS年成長率有機會超過230%；此外，法人則對SK海力士的預估則更加樂觀，持續看好HBM帶來的營收獲利成長效益！國泰台韓科ETF（00735）最大優勢就是為目前全台唯一且獨家一檔打包台灣加上韓國科技龍頭股的海外型ETF，ETF前三大成分股分別是韓國的三星、台灣的台積電和韓國第二大半導體記憶體晶片製造商的SK海力士，三檔權重占比就近六成，其中有四成都是韓國記憶體龍頭，精準捕捉記憶體上漲週期的優質標的。
投信投顧公會最新數據指出，00735近3、6個月及1、2、3年績效展現好成績，分別達到26.38%、73.64%、90.38%、137.20%及188.60%，其中6個月及1年為同類型ETF報酬最佳者，在AI長線商機中，成為最大受惠者之一。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股馬年開盤展現強勢上漲力道，台積電盤中創1,935元新天價，韓股亦創新高。
• 國泰台韓科技ETF(00735)近一年報酬高達90.38%，成為績效冠軍，主要受惠於韓國記憶體大廠三星和SK海力士。
• 三星電子已量產第六代高頻寬記憶體(HBM4)，並搭上輝達新一代架構，預計EPS年成長率超過230%。
• 00735為台灣唯一同時納入台、韓科技龍頭的ETF，前三大成分股為三星、台積電、SK海力士，精準捕捉記憶體上漲週期。
news.Keywords: 台股ETF, 國泰台韓科技ETF, 00735, 記憶體, HBM

news.Title: Morgan Stanley resets Nvidia stock forecast ahead of earnings
news.Content:
Investors are eagerly waiting for Nvidia 's ( NVDA ) Q4 earnings , scheduled for February 25. The last earnings report was an absolute smasher, yet the stock market reacted negatively.

The stock closed at $186.52 on the day of earnings and closed at $180.64 on the next day. I covered analysts’ reactions to those earnings in my article “ Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan and BofA drop verdicts on Nvidia earnings .”

The situation feels a lot different this time. Last earnings were after Nvidia started its downward trend after hitting its peak closing price of $207.04 on October 29, according to Yahoo Finance .

This time, it is approaching earnings after recovering from a sharp drop, closing at $171.88 on February 5.

It looks like the AI bubble fears have taken their toll and are subsiding, and the hope is that, aside from an earnings smasher, incoming Blackwell -trained models will reignite the high expectations for future AI models. Also, the approaching Vera Rubin launch helps.

Revenue is expected to be $65.0 billion, plus or minus 2%, as indicated in the Q3 earnings report .

GAAP gross margins are expected to be 74.8% plus or minus 0.5%.

GAAP operating expenses are expected to be approximately $6.7 billion.

Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore and his team expect strong Q4 results from Nvidia, and have very high confidence that the company will perform well for the full year.

Analysts, who said the last quarter was very robust, with $3 billion of upside and $10 billion of sequential revenue growth, were disappointed by the stock market reaction.
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) is approaching its Q4 earnings report with expectations for strong results, following a previous earnings beat that saw a negative stock reaction.
• The stock has recovered from a sharp drop and is approaching earnings after subsiding AI bubble fears.
• Anticipation for Blackwell-trained models and the Vera Rubin launch may reignite high expectations.
• Q4 revenue is expected to be around $65.0 billion, with GAAP gross margins around 74.8%.
• Morgan Stanley analysts express high confidence in Nvidia's Q4 performance and full-year outlook.
news.Keywords: Earnings Report, AI Models, Revenue Forecast, Gross Margin, Market Sentiment

news.Title: 台積電領軍創高 上市櫃半導體股嗨翻天 逾18檔漲停
news.Content:
台積電（2330）今（24）日股價最高衝上1,975元，市值登上51兆元，來到51.22兆元，股價與市值同譜新高，並帶動上市櫃半導體類股指數齊步大漲約3.5％，盤中共有約18檔上市櫃半導體股衝上漲停，估計今天上市櫃半導體市值大增2兆～3兆元。
上市半導體權值股今天全面動起來，除了台積電之外，日月光投控、聯電等都大漲6％～8％以上，更有光罩、力積電、新唐、景碩、訊芯-KY、凌航及旺宏等漲停，上櫃更有世界、萬潤、穩懋、德微、應廣、捷創科技、雅特力-KY、雍智科技、晶宏、竑 騰、太欣等攻上漲停。
半導體股族群今天全面大噴發，力旺大漲超過9％，群聯、環宇-KY、宏捷科、華景電、九齊、采鈺、晶豪科、易華電、聯鈞等都大漲超過6％。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台積電(2330)股價今日最高衝上1,975元，市值登上51.22兆元，帶動上市櫃半導體類股指數大漲約3.5%。
• 盤中共有約18檔上市櫃半導體股衝上漲停，估計上市櫃半導體市值大增2兆～3兆元。
• 日月光投控、聯電等權值股也大漲6%～8%以上，光罩、力積電、新唐、景碩等漲停。
• 半導體股族群全面噴發，力旺、群聯、環宇-KY等也大漲超過6%。
news.Keywords: 台積電, 半導體股, 市值, 漲停, AI趨勢

news.Title: 台積電飆近2千元大關 創造9777位億萬富翁
news.Content: 台積電（2330）金馬年春開春第二天，今（24）日盤中再度改寫1975元的史上新天價，直逼2000元大關，股東若持有50張以上市值已經接近1億元。據集保結算所資料統計，台積電已創造9777位身價破億元的億萬富翁，「鍍鑽」成為名符其實的台積新貴族、台積新富豪，一年半以來增加超過4077人之多。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台積電(2330)金馬年春開盤第二天，盤中再度改寫1975元的史上新天價，直逼2000元大關。
• 據集保結算所資料統計，台積電已創造9777位身價破億元的億萬富翁。
• 一年半以來，台積電股東人數增加超過4077人，成為「台積新貴族」。
• 此現象反映了長期投資台積電的豐厚回報，以及市場對其未來發展的信心。
news.Keywords: 台積電, 股價天價, 億萬富翁, 股東人數, 長期投資

news.Title: If You'd Invested $1,000 in NVDA 10 Years Ago, Here's How Much You'd Have Today
news.Content:
Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has unquestionably been one of the market's greatest success stories over the past decade. Riding huge semiconductor demand for the artificial intelligence (AI) buildout, it has become the biggest company in the world, with a market cap of around $4.6 trillion.

It's also delivered massive returns for investors. Investors lucky enough to have bought the stock 10 years ago and ridden out the volatility would be sitting on many times their original investment.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

On Feb. 19, 2016, Nvidia was trading at a split-adjusted price of around $0.76. Ten years later, it's trading at $187.95. That translates to a total return over that 10-year time frame (with minor dividends included) of around 25,440%!

NVDA Total Return Price data by YCharts

That means a $1,000 investment in Nvidia 10 years ago would be worth $255,400. That translates to an average annual return of roughly 74%.

Nvidia investors, however, have run into something slightly unusual over the past six months -- a lack of huge returns!

While the stock is still up 8% over that time, it's a far cry from what they have become accustomed to. It's important to remember, though, that the AI revolution is still in its early stages. Nvidia's chip demand should remain robust for the foreseeable future, and that could lead to solid returns for years to come, although maybe not to the level we've seen over the past decade.
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) has delivered massive returns over the past decade, becoming the world's largest company with a market cap of $4.6 trillion.
• A $1,000 investment 10 years ago would be worth approximately $255,400, representing a ~74% average annual return.
• Over the past six months, the stock has seen more modest returns (8%), a contrast to its historical performance.
• The AI revolution is still in its early stages, suggesting continued robust chip demand and potential for solid future returns.
• While past returns may not be repeatable, long-term AI demand supports continued growth.
news.Keywords: Historical Returns, Market Cap Growth, AI Revolution, Semiconductor Demand, Investor Returns

news.Title: 不甩美股! 台股開高走高 早盤漲逾800點 台積電創1975天價
news.Content:
財經中心／唐詩晴、李奇 台北報導
美股道瓊指數，受到關稅不確定性衝擊，昨晚重挫超過800點，但台股今日表現，卻出奇強勁，開盤直接跳空大漲247點，盤中漲超過800點，站穩三萬四千點大關，其中護國神山台積電回神，開漲15塊，盤中甚至再爆1975元新天價。
民視記者 唐詩晴：「不甩美股四大指數全面收黑，台股24號開盤氣勢如虹，開漲247點，而且指數持續向上攻堅，再創新高，不到一小時，又再漲超過600多點。」
就算道瓊下跌821點，但台積電ADR相對抗跌，僅小跌0.13%，報每股370.04美元，讓台股吃了定心丸，脫鉤美股，真的沒在怕，34020點開出，指數開高走高，穩穩力守34K。
加權指數早盤穩穩攻堅，持續創高。（圖／民視新聞）
資深分析師陳建誠：「法人還是非常看好，今年整個AI所帶動的科技趨勢，台積電今年的營收，也是有機會呈現雙位數的成長，以現在目前的成交量，其實還是非常的熱絡，如果這個行情還是每天可以延續在這個8、9千億的成交量的話，那我想到了這個3月，陸陸續續會有這個董事會，會宣布這個公布去年的股利的發放，那今年上半年還會有非常熱絡的這個除權期的行情。」
台積電紅盤開出，光罩早盤就亮燈漲停。（圖／民視新聞）
台積電開漲15元，報1935元，盤中股價再創1,975元新天價，點火大盤，更和台達電、鴻海、聯發科、日月光等電子股帶頭衝。AI資料中心數據傳輸量暴增，帶動矽光子族群，穩懋、華星光五檔，齊刷漲停，延長處置的波若威漲停，還在處置中的聯亞、光聖、環宇-KY等4檔，紅盤之上強勢整理，持續創高。
資深分析師陳建誠：「成熟製程IC這兩年，是相對股價受到壓抑的，那從去年初的這個記憶體的低點，像南亞科、華邦電已經從去年的低點，漲了一年到現在的股價，都來到這個歷史新高，接下來的這個漲價的這個火，會再燒到成熟製程的IC，不管是屬於車用的IC，或者MCU等等。」
指標族群，實力堅強，量能持續放大，分析師提醒，切勿反向做空，短線可低接高出，或把資金分成兩套，分批進場，穩健操作。
原文出處： 不甩美股! 台股開高走高 早盤漲逾800點 台積電創1975天價
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日開盤跳空大漲247點，盤中漲超過800點，站穩三萬四千點大關，台積電盤中再爆1975元新天價。
• 儘管美股四大指數全面收黑，台股展現抗跌力道，脫鉤美股走勢。
• 分析師看好今年AI所帶動的科技趨勢，台積電今年營收有望呈現雙位數成長。
• 記憶體、成熟製程IC等族群表現強勢，預期將延續漲價趨勢。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 台積電, AI趨勢, 記憶體, 成熟製程

news.Title: 台股3個月漲3成！「主動式ETF」00988A超車大盤  股價創掛牌新高
news.Content: 台股今（24）日勢不可擋，盤中觸及34,677點再創新高，從去年11月21日的低點來算，加權指數短短三個月內強彈31%。在此波多頭行情中，「主動式ETF」（00988A） 反彈幅度力壓大盤之外，今日股價最高觸及 12.9 元，創下掛牌以來新高紀錄。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日盤中觸及34,677點再創新高，從去年11月21日低點算起，加權指數三個月內強彈31%。
• 「主動式ETF」（00988A）反彈幅度力壓大盤，今日股價最高觸及12.9元，創下掛牌以來新高紀錄。
• 此波多頭行情中，主動式ETF表現亮眼，顯示其靈活選股策略受到市場青睞。
• 專家看好台股多頭趨勢延續，AI供應鏈仍是主要動能來源。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 主動式ETF, 00988A, 歷史新高, AI供應鏈

news.Title: Tesla loses crucial Autopilot ruling that could cost hundreds of millions
news.Content:
Tesla rolled the dice with a lawsuit it thought it could win easily last year, but it struck out after a U.S. judge upheld the $243 million jury verdict against the company over a fatal crash in southern Florida last year involving the now-defunct Autopilot.

Last August, a Florida jury ruled that the family of Naibel Benavides and crash survivor Dillon Angulo were entitled to the nearly quarter-billion-dollar award after driver George McGee crashed his Tesla into a vehicle they were standing outside of.

McGee testified that he had Autopilot engaged when he killed the 22-year-old Benavides in Key Largo in 2019, but that his eyes were also off the road while he looked for the cellphone he had dropped.

While Tesla argued that data showed McGee had his foot on the accelerator, overriding Autopilot, in the moments before his vehicle crashed at over 60 mph, the jury found Tesla 33% responsible for the crash.

"Tesla in the showroom tells you that they’ve invented the greatest full self-driving car the world has ever seen," Brett Schrieber, the plaintiffs' lawsuit litigator, said at the time . "Mr. Musk has been peddling to consumers and investors for more than a decade that the cars are fully self-driving and that the hardware is capable of full autonomy. And those statements were as untrue the day he said them as they remain untrue today.”

U.S. District Judge Beth Bloom affirmed the jury's initial verdict last Friday, Feb. 20, saying that the evidence at trial "more than supports" the judgment and that Tesla raised no new arguments to warrant reconsideration.

Tesla is expected to appeal the ruling after failing to overturn the award judgment, according to Reuters .
news.AI_Summary:
• A U.S. judge upheld a $243 million jury verdict against Tesla (TSLA) in a fatal crash case involving Autopilot.
• The jury found Tesla 33% responsible for the 2019 crash where the driver reportedly had Autopilot engaged but his eyes off the road.
• Plaintiffs' litigator argued Tesla's claims about its cars being fully self-driving were untrue.
• Tesla is expected to appeal the ruling after failing to overturn the judgment.
• This ruling could have significant financial implications for the company.
news.Keywords: Autopilot Litigation, Jury Verdict, Product Liability, Autonomous Driving Claims, Legal Ruling

news.Title: 高價股強強滾 台積電戰2千 竑騰漲停登千金 漢唐、竹陞備位
news.Content:
台股不甩美股拉回，攜手亞股中的陸日韓等股市齊步大漲，加權指數衝鋒，近午盤一度飆漲900點，指數來到34,688點，站穩34K，而上市櫃千金股也出現34K盛況，竑騰（7751）攻上漲停1,005元，至午盤時，台股維持34千金不墜。
台股今天（24日）穩穩的出現34千金，權王台積電領頭扮功臣，今天早盤最高攻上1,975元大漲75元，蓄勢挑戰2,000元大關，而群聯今天再度站上2,000元，推升2,000元俱樂部有15檔，也是歷史首見盛況。
除了34千金之外，漢唐、竹陞今天股價也衝上980元以上，備位挑戰千元，若這2檔股價成功重返千元，台股將有機會挑戰36千金。
台股不只千金股百花齊放，高價股都強強滾，雍智科技股價衝上910元歷史新高，華城、高力、旭隼等都是900元俱樂部。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股不甩美股拉回，攜手亞股股市同步大漲，加權指數衝鋒，盤中一度飆漲900點，來到34,688點。
• 上市櫃千金股出現34家盛況，竑騰攻上漲停1,005元，台股維持34千金不墜。
• 權王台積電領頭扮功臣，早盤最高攻上1,975元，蓄勢挑戰2,000元大關。
• 高價股強強滾，雍智科技衝上910元歷史新高，華城、高力、旭隼等都是900元俱樂部。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 千金股, 台積電, 高價股, AI趨勢

news.Title: 多頭猛攻 台指期早盤劍指35K
news.Content:
台指期24日延續多頭行情，高點暫報34,906點，成交口數約5.81萬口；電子期上漲67.1點，報價2,155.2點；金融期下跌5.8點，成交價2,573點，微台指成交價34,847，上漲876點；小台指期上漲880點，暫報34,851點；台灣中型100期貨成交價28,151點，上漲493點。
在美股走勢部份，市場憂慮人工智慧對產業的衝擊，再加上關稅不確定性影響，美股四大指數同步走跌，終場道瓊大跌 1.66%，標普 500 下跌 1.04%，那斯達克下跌 1.13%，費城半導體下跌 0.57%，輝達上漲 0.91%，台積電 ADR小跌 0.12%。
凱基投顧表示，儘管美國川普總統祭出 122條款關稅，由於 122 條款課徵的 15%稅率與台灣原先協議(ART)的稅率接近，關稅不確定性因素可望降低。且根據過去 10 年經驗，台股在開紅盤後往往呈現「逐步走高」格局，後 5 日、10 日、20 日上漲機率分別為 70%、80%、85%，顯示年後資金歸隊意願強烈，多頭行情具備延續性。操作上可持續鎖定具漲價題材與均線多頭強勢股，並趁盤中震盪之際布局價差機會。
關注其它期貨交易情況，臺幣黃金期貨成交價19,695點，上漲123.5點、布蘭特原油期貨成交價2,263點，上漲30.5點、富櫃200期貨參考價13,154點、臺灣永續期貨成交價18,067點，上漲329點、臺灣生技期貨成交價4,141點，上漲37點、半導體30期貨成交價11,075點，上漲519點、航運期貨參考價183.1點。
國外期貨部分，美國那斯達克100期貨成交價24,856點，下跌450點、美國費城半導體期貨成交價8,152點，下跌17點；個股期貨方面，台積電期貨報價1,975元，上漲60元、鴻海期貨上漲3.5元，報價232.5元、聯發科期貨上漲15元至1,820元、長榮期貨上漲1元，報價191元；緯創期貨上漲0.5元，報價130.5元。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台指期24日延續多頭行情，高點暫報34,906點，成交口數約5.81萬口。
• 儘管美股四大指數走跌，但台指期夜盤仍以小漲作收，顯示市場情緒觀望但具韌性。
• 凱基投顧認為，關稅不確定性因素可望降低，台股開紅盤後往往呈現逐步走高格局。
• 操作上可持續鎖定具漲價題材與均線多頭強勢股，並趁盤中震盪之際布局價差機會。

news.Title: 台積電衝上1975元歷史新高貢獻台股近600漲點 市值飆出51.2兆元
news.Content:
台股今 (24) 日盤中飆漲超過 900 點，權王台積電 (2330-TW)(TSM-US) 大漲近 4%，一度衝上 1975 元，再創歷史新高，市值也飆上 51.22 兆元，貢獻大盤指數近 600 點。
台積電受惠 AI 晶片需求強勁，先進製程與先進封裝產能供不應求，第一季美元中位數營收將季增 4%，續創歷史新高，且毛利率、營益率將同步超越上季數值，雙率再締造新猷。
以整體產業環境來看，台積電預期，晶圓製造 2.0 產業將在 2026 年成長 14%，受惠於強勁的 AI 相關需求，而台積電憑藉市場對先進製程、特殊製程和先進封裝技術的強勁需求，成長幅度將繼續超越產業，預計 2026 年將又是強勁成長的一年，若以美元計，全年營收預計成長接近 30%。
台積電已上調五年營收成長率，以美元計算，2024 年至 2029 年五年間，公司整體長期營收年複合成長率接近 25%，相較先前預估的近 20% 提升 5 個百分點。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日盤中飆漲超過900點，權王台積電(2330)大漲近4%，一度衝上1975元，再創歷史新高。
• 台積電市值也飆上51.22兆元，貢獻大盤指數近600點。
• 台積電受惠AI晶片需求強勁，預期2026年全年美元營收成長近30%，五年營收成長率上調至近25%。
• 台積電預期2026年晶圓代工產業營收將年增14%，成長幅度將繼續超越產業。
news.Keywords: 台積電, 股價創新高, 市值, AI晶片, 營收成長

news.Title: 台股收漲927點今年最大、市值首破113兆元！網友驚呼　專家：有望續強
news.Content: 台股今（24）日在權值股台積電(2330）、台達電（2308）帶動下開高走高，收盤以34700點作收，上漲927點，漲點創今年最大、史上第二大，上市公司總市值首度站上113兆元。市場多頭氣氛沸騰引發網友熱議，有人直呼「上看8到10萬，好心才告訴你」。對此，專家表示，目前仍屬強勢多頭格局，持續上漲機率大。

• 權值股台積電(2330）、台達電（2308）帶動下開高走高，市場多頭氣氛沸騰。

news.Title: Below $400 Again, Is Tesla Stock a Buy?
news.Content:
Shares of electric-vehicle and energy specialist Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) are back below $400 as of this writing. Additionally, the stock is down about 12% year to date, even as Tesla keeps leaning harder into autonomy and a shift toward a more software- and services-driven model.

A pullback like this can look tempting given that Tesla is investing aggressively in promising growth initiatives. Its Robotaxi service, in particular, could morph into a major business for the company over the long haul. But the question is whether the economics of Tesla's newer, higher-margin initiatives will show up quickly enough -- and with enough durability -- to justify today's valuation.

Where to invest $1,000 right now? Our analyst team just revealed what they believe are the 10 best stocks to buy right now, when you join Stock Advisor. See the stocks »

Robotaxi is an autonomous ride-hailing platform using Tesla's technology and vehicles. Launched in June 2025, the service currently operates primarily with the company's Model Y vehicles, and is expected to increasingly rely on its upcoming, purpose-built autonomous Cybercab over time.

Interestingly, with the stock trading at a price-to-earnings ratio of about 370, investors seem to already be pricing in a successful launch of Robotaxi at scale. In other words, the current valuation prices in not just an automaker with a successful energy storage business (the Tesla we know today), but also newer initiatives. Put another way, the current valuation is arguably asking investors to underwrite a company that can build and run a scaled fleet-based service business while also selling self-driving software.

Of course, Tesla has been making notable progress on these newer initiatives. So it makes sense for investors to start considering them. In 2025, for instance, Tesla's self-driving software capabilities dramatically improved, and it began installing production lines for Cybercab. Additionally, in its fourth-quarter update, the company outlined a planned Robotaxi coverage roadmap that starts in Austin and expands to additional U.S. metros in the first half of 2026.

Execution of its plans, of course, will be the hard part. Robotaxi may prove to be more complex than investors expect. Running a ride-hailing network requires operational infrastructure that a pure software company never has to touch. In its recent annual report, Tesla itself called out the work behind the scenes, including vehicle cleaning and maintenance, charging, security, teleoperations, and fleet management. Those are real costs, and they tend to scale with miles driven, not just with software subscriptions.

Even more, the company's current growth initiatives are capital-intensive.

"We currently expect our capital expenditures to be in excess of $20 billion in 2026," Tesla said in its 2025 annual report, pointing to AI initiatives like compute infrastructure and data centers, manufacturing and R&D expansion, and growth in its company-operated AI-enabled asset fleet.

That is a dramatic step-up. For context, Tesla's capital expenditures were $8.5 billion in 2025. In other words, management is guiding toward a year in which capital spending more than doubles.

So, yes: the company is investing in some exciting growth initiatives. But it's easier said than done. Tesla is trying to scale autonomy, AI infrastructure, and fleet operations simultaneously. Those initiatives may create a larger profit pool later, but they require heavy spending now.

But as hard as it may be for Tesla to succeed in these new initiatives, their initial success is already being priced into the growth stock. With a price-to-earnings ratio approaching 400, investors have priced in the expectation that profits will expand sharply as autonomy and software scale, while the core vehicle business returns to growth.

There is also a timing risk that arguably doesn't get enough attention in bull cases. Tesla can be right about the long-term direction and still be early. Regulation, consumer adoption, safety performance, and the practical cost of operating a fleet can all slow the timeline -- and a valuation like this gives investors little cushion if the timeline slips.

Tesla is doing ambitious work. I'm just not buying the stock below $400 today, because the spending ramp and the valuation together make for an unattractive setup in my opinion.

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $492,341 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $50,381 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $424,262 !*

Right now, we’re issuing “Double Down” alerts for three incredible companies , available when you join Stock Advisor , and there may not be another chance like this anytime soon.

See the 3 stocks »

*Stock Advisor returns as of February 23, 2026

Daniel Sparks and/or his clients have positions in Tesla. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Tesla. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

Below $400 Again, Is Tesla Stock a Buy? was originally published by The Motley Fool
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla (TSLA) shares are trading below $400, down ~12% year-to-date, despite investments in autonomy and software services.
• The company's Robotaxi service, using Model Y and upcoming Cybercab, could become a major business.
• The stock's high P/E ratio (~370) suggests investors are already pricing in a successful Robotaxi launch at scale.
• Tesla plans significant capital expenditures, exceeding $20 billion in 2026, up from $8.5 billion in 2025, for AI and fleet operations.
• The valuation implies investors are underwriting a company that can build and run a scaled fleet-based service business alongside selling self-driving software.
news.Keywords: Robotaxi, Autonomous Driving, Capital Expenditures, Valuation, Software Services

news.Title: 晶圓代工領軍，ABF、記憶體、被動元件等群雄並起，台股再創新高
news.Content:
【財訊快報／方亞申】關稅議題壓抑市場氣氛，美國總統川普週一再度強調其提高關稅的能力，並警告若有國家「耍花樣」將面臨更高關稅。此外，市場再度同時受到人工智慧(AI)衝擊疑慮，軟體再度受重創，美股週一道瓊指數狂瀉逾800點，標普500指數下跌1%，那斯達克綜合指數下跌1.1%。科技股除了光纖、輝達、半導體設備、零售、能源外，普遍下跌；週二亞洲股市卻又在韓國記憶體、日本電子原料、半導體相關股拉抬下，除香港外大都上漲，台股在ABF、玻纖布、被動元件、記憶體、衛星以及台積電(2330)、日月光(3711)、台達電(2308)、台塑集團股拉抬指數一度上漲超過850點，逼近34700點，但預估成交量不到9千億元，有點可惜。美股週一大跌，延續美國最高法院上週否決其「對等關稅」後，川普已於上週六表示，將全球關稅自10%提高至15%，並稱新關稅將立即生效，但相關正式文件時程仍不明確，未來數月也可能推出更多關稅措施。不穩定狀況使得道瓊回測季線、標普及那指跌破季線，都有拉長整理時間味道。費半雖還在月線之上，主要是記憶體設備支撐，一旦輝達在下去則費半指數也要向季線測試支撐。其實美股回檔也算是自去年4月中上旬大漲以來技術性拉回。
亞洲的日韓股市由於有記憶體、玻纖布、PCB鑽頭、半導體設備、被動元件等AI原物料及零組件，表現較強，韓國證指向6000點，日本也向新高挑戰，表現最強。中國受國稅利多影響反彈，倒是港股偏弱勢。國際黃金、白銀、油價都再度反彈，令人不得不注意。
台股週一以電子次族群記憶體、ABF、玻纖布、矽光子相關族群表現最強，南亞(1303)及金融股也出現少見強勢，拉高指數，雖留上影線融資也增加47億元，不過週二ABF四家除臻鼎-KY(4958)外，南電(8046)量縮漲停，其次景碩(3189)、欣興(3037)也跟上，外資高喊超級循環，要到2028年才能滿足供給，是台股最強電子次族群。其次美國及日本光纖股諸如LITE、康寧、古河電工等表現後來居上強勢，使得台股相關的波若威(3163)、華星光(4979)、上詮(3363)、統新(6426)、聯亞(3081)表現都很強；而日東紡織、村田、太陽誘電更強，刺激南亞(1303)、台玻(1802)、華新科(2492)、國巨*(2327)、禾伸堂(3026)、日電貿(3090)、九豪(6127)、華容(5328)、三集瑞-KY(6862)、凱美(2375)、台慶科(3357)等全數大漲。玻纖布的台玻(1802)、富喬(1815)、建榮(5340)、德宏(5475)都大漲。
衛星相關股啟碁(6285)、華通(2313)、昇達科(3491)、錸德(2349)、耀登(3138)以及記憶體旺宏(2337)、力積電(6770)、群聯(8299)、南亞科(2408)等續漲可延均線操作。台積電大聯盟萬潤(6187)、長興材料(5234)、中砂(1560)、漢唐(2404)以及封測的日月光(3711)、京元電子(2449)、欣銓(3264)、東華(8110)乃至世界(5347)、聯電(2303)都很強。
傳產以南亞、台玻及華新(1605)為主，金融彰銀(2801)、凱基金(2883)、元大金(2885)表現較強，整體以金三退二為主。
台股指數再創歷史新高，台積電是中流砥柱，超級循環的記憶體、ABF、矽光子、衛星等次族群是強弱指標。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日指數一度上漲超過850點，逼近34700點，再創歷史新高，惟預估成交量不到9千億元。
• 台積電是中流砥柱，超級循環的記憶體、ABF、矽光子、衛星等次族群是強弱指標。
• 美國關稅議題及AI衝擊疑慮未能影響台股多頭氣勢，亞洲股市普遍上漲。
• 建議持續鎖定AI概念股、先進製程、太空衛星、被動元件、ABF、光通訊及PCB相關族群。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 歷史新高, 台積電, AI概念股, 記憶體

news.Title: 《台北股市》美科技巨頭狂撒21兆 專家點3族群漲最兇
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】美國大型科技股財報出爐，2026年超大規模資料中心資本支出上看6,460億美元，引爆AI基礎建設熱潮。資深分析師指出，龐大算力需求導致上游供應鏈產能吃緊，記憶體與台積電(2330)先進製程面臨嚴重短缺，預期台股2026年多頭趨勢不變，AI供應鏈仍是主流，建議聚焦先進製程、AI關鍵零組件及缺貨漲價三大方向，伺機布局AI績優股。
據APOLLO統計，2026年美國超大規模資料中心的6,460億美元資本支出，規模約占美國GDP的2%，不僅超越奧地利、泰國及挪威等國的GDP，更與比利時、丹麥和印尼股市的市值相當，甚至大於德、法、英、日等多國軍事開支總和。摩爾投顧分析師葉俊敏分析，科技巨擘之所以願意投入如此龐大的資金，主要歸因於AI大模型的算力需求，而基礎建設的大規模成長，也直接導致了上游供應鏈的產能瓶頸。
隨AI模型複雜度攀升，DeepMind執行長哈薩比斯日前即點出，硬體限制將影響AI部署，其中記憶體正面臨嚴重的供應短缺。短缺效應同樣蔓延至晶圓代工端，台積電(2330)先進製程供不應求，促使其2026年資本支出預估調升至520億至560億美元。
產能規畫上，3奈米月產能預估2026年底達20萬片，2奈米月產能上看10萬片；同時，美國亞利桑那廠3奈米預計2027年量產，日本熊本二廠亦升級為3奈米製程。輝達(NVDA)執行長黃仁勳更預估，未來10年內台積電產能恐須增加逾100%，凸顯市場強烈需求帶動了台積電的積極擴產。
針對台股後市，葉俊敏表示，台股新春開紅盤後再創歷史新高皆在預期之內，展望2026年，將維持多頭趨勢，並以AI供應鏈為絕對主流；他提醒投資人可持續關注先進製程、AI關鍵零組件缺貨漲價等三大題材方向，耐心等待相關AI產業績優股的買進訊號。
◎AI產業績優股，給投資人參考：
先進製程：中砂(1560)、昇陽半(8028)、萬潤(6187)、志聖(2467)、竑騰(7751)
特化：新應材(4749)、台特化(4772)、長興(1717)、達興材(5234)
CCL：富喬(1815)、台玻(1802)、金居(8358)、榮科(4989)、台光電(2383)、台燿(6274)
CPO：上詮(3363)、波若威(3163)、眾達-KY(4977)、聯亞(3081)、環宇-KY(4991)、IET-KY(4971)
◎個股詳細解析：
中砂(1560)：半導體鑽石碟與再生晶圓大廠。3奈米、2奈米等先進製程市佔率極高。
竑騰(7751)：半導體後段封裝設備廠，產品包括點膠植片散熱機、AOI檢測機等。

新應材(4749)：3奈米及2奈米製程的Rinse(表面改質劑)、BARC(底部抗反射層)及EBR(洗邊劑)。
富喬(1815)：高階玻纖布供應商，具備垂直一貫化製程能力。高階LowCTE玻纖布，切入AI伺服器及先進封裝市場。

台燿(6274)：GoogleTPUv7/v8與AWSASIC訂單。高階占比提升毛利率。
上詮(3363)：CPO和矽光子(SiPh)技術，提供解決方案。台積電合作夥伴。
環宇-KY(4991)：4吋InP晶圓廠產能。主要客戶包含：Innolight、Lumentum及Coherent。
眾達-KY(4977)：與博通等美系大廠緊密合作CPO技術。外部光源(ELS)產品已進入量產階段。(新聞來源：旺得富理財網 李宸緯)

news.Title: 《台北股市》美科技巨頭狂撒21兆 專家點3族群漲最兇
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】美國大型科技股財報出爐，2026年超大規模資料中心資本支出上看6,460億美元，引爆AI基礎建設熱潮。資深分析師指出，龐大算力需求導致上游供應鏈產能吃緊，記憶體與台積電(2330)先進製程面臨嚴重短缺，預期台股2026年多頭趨勢不變，AI供應鏈仍是主流，建議聚焦先進製程、AI關鍵零組件及缺貨漲價三大方向，伺機布局AI績優股。
據APOLLO統計，2026年美國超大規模資料中心的6,460億美元資本支出，規模約占美國GDP的2%，不僅超越奧地利、泰國及挪威等國的GDP，更與比利時、丹麥和印尼股市的市值相當，甚至大於德、法、英、日等多國軍事開支總和。摩爾投顧分析師葉俊敏分析，科技巨擘之所以願意投入如此龐大的資金，主要歸因於AI大模型的算力需求，而基礎建設的大規模成長，也直接導致了上游供應鏈的產能瓶頸。
隨AI模型複雜度攀升，DeepMind執行長哈薩比斯日前即點出，硬體限制將影響AI部署，其中記憶體正面臨嚴重的供應短缺。短缺效應同樣蔓延至晶圓代工端，台積電(2330)先進製程供不應求，促使其2026年資本支出預估調升至520億至560億美元。
產能規畫上，3奈米月產能預估2026年底達20萬片，2奈米月產能上看10萬片；同時，美國亞利桑那廠3奈米預計2027年量產，日本熊本二廠亦升級為3奈米製程。輝達(NVDA)執行長黃仁勳更預估，未來10年內台積電產能恐須增加逾100%，凸顯市場強烈需求帶動了台積電的積極擴產。
針對台股後市，葉俊敏表示，台股新春開紅盤後再創歷史新高皆在預期之內，展望2026年，將維持多頭趨勢，並以AI供應鏈為絕對主流；他提醒投資人可持續關注先進製程、AI關鍵零組件缺貨漲價等三大題材方向，耐心等待相關AI產業績優股的買進訊號。
◎AI產業績優股，給投資人參考：
先進製程：中砂(1560)、昇陽半(8028)、萬潤(6187)、志聖(2467)、竑騰(7751)
特化：新應材(4749)、台特化(4772)、長興(1717)、達興材(5234)
CCL：富喬(1815)、台玻(1802)、金居(8358)、榮科(4989)、台光電(2383)、台燿(6274)
CPO：上詮(3363)、波若威(3163)、眾達-KY(4977)、聯亞(3081)、環宇-KY(4991)、IET-KY(4971)
◎個股詳細解析：
中砂(1560)：半導體鑽石碟與再生晶圓大廠。3奈米、2奈米等先進製程市佔率極高。
竑騰(7751)：半導體後段封裝設備廠，產品包括點膠植片散熱機、AOI檢測機等。
長興(1717)：全球最大的乾膜光阻供應商，產品橫跨合成樹脂、電子材料及特用化學品。先進封裝用的封裝材料。
新應材(4749)：3奈米及2奈米製程的Rinse(表面改質劑)、BARC(底部抗反射層)及EBR(洗邊劑)。
富喬(1815)：高階玻纖布供應商，具備垂直一貫化製程能力。高階LowCTE玻纖布，切入AI伺服器及先進封裝市場。
金居(8358)：PCB上游電解銅箔大廠，專攻伺服器、網通等高頻高速應用。
台燿(6274)：GoogleTPUv7/v8與AWSASIC訂單。高階占比提升毛利率。
上詮(3363)：CPO和矽光子(SiPh)技術，提供解決方案。台積電合作夥伴。
環宇-KY(4991)：4吋InP晶圓廠產能。主要客戶包含：Innolight、Lumentum及Coherent。
眾達-KY(4977)：與博通等美系大廠緊密合作CPO技術。外部光源(ELS)產品已進入量產階段。(新聞來源：旺得富理財網 李宸緯)

news.Title: 《半導體》華邦電被瘋買 驚動老牌投顧 14天上修目標價
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股封關期間，美光、三星、SK海力士大漲，帶動台記憶體股開紅盤補漲，指標股華邦電更連2天漲停，昨(2/23)躍10大成交人氣股，也是投信買超前3大，估砸4.92億元瘋買4279張，是南亞科(2408)的3倍量，加上SK海力士庫存拉警報，本土老牌投顧認為，記憶體供需偏緊，報價維持高檔，將華邦電從2周前的103.5元上修至150元，投資評等也從區間操作升至買進。
本土老牌投顧過年前才出具華邦電報告，僅短短2周就更新報告，將華邦電(2344)目標價從103.5元上修4成至150元，今(2/24)盤中一度觸漲停122元，後打開至121元，以此推算約還有24%漲幅。
投顧強調，AI發展改變產業追求性價比與記憶體單價的傳統，轉而重視效能、算力及大容量。記憶體市場供需出現結構性改變，華邦電客戶的訂單動能明顯轉強，並開始主動洽談長期合作，反映出對利基型DRAM需求強勁，Flash需求亦同步回溫。
年後部份供應商暫停報價，顯示供給端強勢
在AI需求持續擴張，排擠非AI記憶體的產能，其中DDR4價格漲勢尤為明顯。2026第1季DDR4 8Gb合約價可望季增90%以上，市場供需結構處於高度緊繃狀態。預估華邦電2026年稅後EPS為10.02元。現貨價目前處於高檔區間震盪，記憶體產業供需偏緊，基本面具支撐，年後部份供應商仍暫停報價，顯示供給端態度強勢，因此投資建議調升為買進(Buy)，目標價150元(PER15X)。
投顧指出，華邦電2025年資本支出55億元，年減64.5%，預估2026年資本支出將大增至421億元，年增665.5%，其中生產設備資本支出約占95%，以加速增產。台中廠新產能可望於2026年7月投片，高雄廠預計2026年5~6月裝機擴產，新增產能2027年量產。
Flash與CMS擴產計畫正進行中，其中70億左右以擴台中Flash為主，350億元擴充CMS。台中廠區規劃月產能自50~51K片，增加到57~58K片，全年位元年增約30%到40%，保留NAND和NOR產能互相轉換的彈性，製程是F45/F24。
華邦電高雄廠目前月產能約15K片，全部用於DRAM生產，其中20nm(奈米)製程約占10K片，其餘25nm製程，預計第1季導入16nm製程，CMS 16nm製程已完成試產前準備，重點產品包括8Gb/16Gb DDR4與8Gb LPDDR4。(新聞來源：旺得富理財網 馮恩惠)
※

• 投信瘋買華邦電4279張，是南亞科的3倍量，加上SK海力士庫存拉警報，記憶體供需偏緊。
• 老牌投顧將華邦電目標價從103.5元上修至150元，投資評等升至買進。

news.Title: WSJ：輝達再次進軍消費PC晶片市場、攜手英特爾與聯發科
news.Content:
華爾街日報報導，人工智慧（AI）晶片巨擘輝達（NVIDIA）即將重返消費型PC市場，內建其筆記型電腦晶片的產品預計今年將由戴爾（Dell）、聯想（Lenovo）等品牌推出。
作為全球市值最高的公司，輝達並不指望能迅速從家用PC晶片中獲取巨額利潤，但分析師表示，在所有設備都將具備AI功能的時代，該公司希望保持與消費者的聯繫。
台灣研究機構Digitimes副總監Jason Tsai表示：「這不只是供應特定晶片或製造更好的零件，而是關於輝達如何更深入地整合進次世代PC生態系。」
輝達及其合作夥伴希望讓PC更輕薄，同時保持長效續航力。分析師表示，這將使搭載微軟Windows作業系統的硬體能更直接地與蘋果最新的MacBook競爭。
在PC晶片方面，輝達有兩項合作案：一項是去年宣布與英特爾（Intel）的合作；另一項則是輝達執行長黃仁勳於1月訪問台灣期間非正式透露與台灣晶片設計商聯發科（MediaTek）的合作。
輝達的新款PC處理器設計為所謂的系統單晶片（SoC），將中央處理器與該公司著名的強大繪圖處理器（GPU）整合在一起，而GPU正是驅動AI模型的晶片。
系統單晶片在iPhone等智慧型手機中已是標準配置，但在PC中尚未普及。輝達晶片的目標是在不犧牲效能的情況下，為PC提供行動設備特有的效率與續航力。黃仁勳將其描述為「低功耗但非常強大」。
知情輝達供應鏈人士透露，包括戴爾、聯想等PC製造商正與輝達合作開發使用輝達-聯發科系統單晶片的機型，該晶片採用英國晶片設計公司安謀（Arm）的架構。他們表示，首批內建該晶片的PC可能會在今年上半推出。
在此同時，輝達也正與掌控約70％ Windows PC晶片市場的英特爾合作。這項合作開發的晶片將就英特爾的中央處理器（CPU）與輝達的繪圖及AI技術進行整合。
news.AI_Summary:
• 華爾街日報報導，輝達（NVIDIA）將重返消費型PC市場，與英特爾及聯發科合作推出內建輝達晶片的筆電。
• 輝達希望透過此舉在AI時代保持與消費者的聯繫，並整合進次世代PC生態系。
• 輝達與聯發科合作的PC晶片採用Arm架構，整合CPU與GPU，目標是提供高效率與長續航力。
• 輝達也與英特爾合作，將自家GPU及AI技術融入英特爾的處理器生態系統。
news.Keywords: 輝達, 聯發科, PC晶片, Arm架構, AI時代

news.Title: 【Hot台股】力積電攻上漲停板！網樂「黃董三位數出發」 分析師揭1關鍵：有難度
news.Content:
今日社群台股最Hot議題 「相信黃董」、「力積電鎖啦 舒服!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!」、「你會想念十位數的黃董！」。
「力G一直鎖不住，你各位要相信黃董阿」、「黃董三位數出發」、「#相信黃董 年後就懂」、「力積電遲遲不鎖就是為了吃貨 好好把握機會吧!!!!!!!」、「力積趕緊鎖一鎖吧」、「力積電燈燈啦~~~~~~~~~~~」、「力積電吃光啦」。
針對網友熱議話題...專家這麼說！ HOT台股今日值班專家－倫元投顧分析師周佳和
周佳和表示，這波記憶體股包括指標股南亞科（2408）、華邦電（3481）股價都很強勢，出現整理後轉強跡象，相關族群也有連動和一致性。力積電則在昨日小漲後，今日亮燈漲停鎖在73.7元，走一個輪動性。
周佳和分析，台美日合力打造下世代AI超級電腦記憶體，並委由台廠力積電試產與製造，讓力積電從一般消費型記憶體，轉向高階記憶體，市場預期在產品組合改變，後續幾個季度毛利率有機會提升。
周佳和指出，力積電在出售銅鑼廠後，今年至少賺逾1股本，以基本面來看目前股價不算貴，建議持有者可續抱，並觀察KD指標是否來到80以上過熱區；空手者則不建議此時追價，待股價震盪回到平台整理區68到70元左右再行介入。
針對網友熱議股價有望來到三位數？周佳和認為，短期內仍困難，闖關百元意味著突破前方90元的大量套牢區，需要長時間消化，至於這一波記憶體行情能否走那麼遠，還待時間觀察。
Hot台股主視覺--內文banner
王詩婷／財經特派記者，主跑勞動部、半導體、生技等
news.AI_Summary:
• 網友熱議力積電(6770)股價表現，認為「你會想念十位數的黃董！」
• 專家認為，力積電在出售銅鑼廠後，今年至少賺逾1股本，股價不算貴，建議持有者續抱。
• 專家認為力積電短期內難以突破百元關卡，需長時間消化套牢區，記憶體行情能否走遠待觀察。
• 力積電受惠於AI超級電腦記憶體試產與製造，產品組合改變有望提升毛利率。
news.Keywords: 力積電, 記憶體, AI超級電腦, 毛利率, 股價預期

news.Title: 台股飆927點創史上第2大漲點 台積電市值增約一間日月光
news.Content: 收盤更新 金馬年開春第2個交易日，台股今早呈現「狂奔」927點，超越今年1月5日創下的755點紀錄，創下史上第二大單日漲點紀錄。其中最大權王台積電（2330）一檔貢獻指數近540點之多，短短一天市值暴增1.68兆元，相當於一間日月光（3711）的規模。

• 最大權王台積電（2330）一檔貢獻指數近540點，市值暴增1.68兆元，相當於一間日月光（3711）的規模。
• 台積電股價盤中衝上1975元新天價，終場收在1965元。

news.Title: 台積電衝1975元新天價！台股受激勵　盤中飆漲近9百點
news.Content:
即時中心／綜合報導
美股受到美國最高法院對關稅裁決影響，加上市場對人工智慧仍有疑慮，週一（23日）全數收跌，道瓊工業指數更大跌逾8百點！台股在農曆年後首個交易日收紅盤，今（24）日開盤率先突破3萬4大關；盤中台積電衝出1,975元新天價，大盤受激勵飆漲898.12點、來到34,671.38點，續寫盤中歷史新高紀錄！
加權指數今開盤上漲247.45點、以34,020.71點開出；隨後台積電衝出1,975元新天價，大盤受激勵不斷拉高，稍早更飆漲898.12點、來到34,671.38點，突破3萬4大關，續寫盤中歷史新高紀錄！
電子權值股持續衝高，台積電（2330）以1,915元開出、上漲15元；隨後續上漲75元，衝出1,975元新天價；台達電（2308）持續上攻，一度報出1,375元天價、大漲70元；日月光投控（3711）、聯電（2303）股價也飆漲逾8％。
《民視新聞網》提醒您：內容僅供參考，投資人於決策時應審慎評估風險，並就投資結果自行負責。
原文出處： 快新聞／台積電衝1975元新天價！台股受激勵 盤中飆漲近9百點
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股24日開盤跳空大漲247點，盤中漲超過800點，站穩三萬四千點大關，台積電盤中再爆1975元新天價。
• 儘管美股四大指數全面收黑，台股展現抗跌力道，脫鉤美股走勢。
• 分析師看好今年AI所帶動的科技趨勢，台積電今年營收有望呈現雙位數成長。
• 記憶體、成熟製程IC等族群表現強勢，預期將延續漲價趨勢。
news.Keywords: 台股指數, 台積電, AI趨勢, 記憶體, 成熟製程
